00-3 Kraavan Medium Freighter: A rugged and reliable cargo freighter, the Kravaan has been operating tirelessly in the Outer Rim for decades. Featuring a large, underbelly cargo hold, it is designed for speed, despite carrying heavy loads.
1-L Imperial-Series Repulsor Tank: Produced for the Imperial Army by Ubrikkian Industries, the 1-L is the lightest and fastest member of the well-respected Imperial series of repulsor tanks.
1.4 FD P-Tower: A cheap, rugged defensive emplacement that can operate in almost any environment, the 1.4 FD P-Tower mounts a heavy laser cannon to take down light vehicles. They are a popular budget defense against pirates. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
16-M Railcrawler Conveyex Transport: The civilian version of the heavily armoured 20-T railcrawler, the 16-M was widely used throughout frontier worlds for efficient transport where security was less of an issue or the value of cargo was low. Despite less armour plating and the lighter frame, it also held a less powerful engine, meaning it barely had a faster top speed than its younger sibling. The Mining Guild has oft used these transports in their operations to reduce costs in hauling the copious amounts of raw materials. The railcrawler is capable of hauling 16-M intermodal cargo pods like the 20-T, but doesn’t have the engine power to pull more than a handful of the armoured pods. However, it can pull any number of the cargo pods built for this model. These each cost 5,000 credits with the same rarity as the drive car, are each Silhouette 4, have Armour 2, and a Hull Trauma Threshold of 18.
20-T Railcrawler Conveyex Transport: The 20-T Railcrawler by Kuat Drive Yards is one of the premier conveyex cargo haulers in the galaxy. Vehicle Type/Model: Conveyex/20-T Railcrawler. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Medium. Crew: One drive, one co-driver/engineer. Encumbrance Capacity: 75. Passenger Capacity: 2. Price/Rarity: 140,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Forward-mounted medium laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 3). *Dorsal turret-mounted twin light laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). Cargo Containers: A Railcrawler can tow any number of intermodal cargo containers. These do not count against its encumbrance capacity or affect its speed and handling. Trackbound: As a rail train, a Railcrawler is severely restricted in its mobility compared to other vehicles.
431 Hauler Cargo Tug: Any pilot who has spent his life near space docks is no stranger to the cargo tug. Simple, and made for pure utility, these working vessels populate many cargo transfer stations, space docks, and loading points. Pilots spend a full shift laboring in the cramped conditions of a simple one-man tug. Not built for comfort, these small crafts have a single purpose in mind, moving space born objects from one location to another. By using standard tractor beam technology, they can lock on to relatively large objects and deliver them to their destination. Though not fast, their engines are designed to deliver consistent performance despite moving a considerable load.
48 Roller Wheel Bike: In a galaxy full of oddities, wheel bikes may be some of the oddest things out there from a technological point of view.
4R3 Light Assault Transport: Officially, the 4R3 is classified as light freighter instead of a military assault craft. Hull Type/Class: Light Assault Transport/4R3. Manufacturer: Telgorn Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 15. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Medium. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot, two gunners, one engineer. Encumbrance: 80. Passenger Capacity: 16. Consumables: One month. Price/Rarity: 300,000/7. Customization Hard Points: 4. Weapons: *Retractable forward mounted medium laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). *Two retractable forward mounted twin light ion cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Ion, Linked 1). *Two forward mounted proton torpedo launchers (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 8; Critical 2; Range [Short]; Blast 6, Breach 6, Guided 2, Limited Ammo 7, Slow-Firing 1).
580-AvA \"Dash\" Speeder Bike: A lesser-known variant of the 610-AvA Dart, the Dash speeder bike is just as easy to use but also features a collapsible frame for easy storage and transportation.
610-AvA \"Dart\" Speeder Bike: The 610-AvA Dart is a civilian version of Aratech's 614-AvA military speeder bike, a predecessor of the famous 74-Y used by the Imperial Army.
614-AvA Speeder Bike: Aratech's 614-AvA is a small, fast, lightweight speeder bike used by the Imperial Armed Forces. Vehicle Type/Model: Speeder Bike/614-AvA. Manufacturer: Aratech Repulsor Company. Maximum Altitude: 20 meters. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 6. Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 4,200 credits (R)/6. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: * Forward-mounted blasters—this weapon's entire profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 12; Critical 2; Range [Long]; Linked 1, Pierce 2). Collapsible: Collapsing or expanding the bike requires a maneuver. When collapsed, the vehicle is a silhouette 1 and cannot be used.
64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorsled: The Swift 3 is a fast, rugged, versatile repulsorsled made by Aratech Repulsor Company. Vehicle Type/Model: Repulsorsled/Swift 3. Manufacturer: Aratech Repulsor Company. Maximum Altitude: 25 meters. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 20. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 8,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 4. Weapons: *Forward-mounted auto-blaster (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 3; Critical 5; Range [Close]; Auto-fire). *Forward-mounted magnetic grapnel launcher (Fire Arc Forward; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Knockdown, Limited Ammo 1, Tractor 3). Similar to the electromagnetic harpoon launcher found on Incom's T-47 airspeeder, the Swift 3's magnetic grapnel launcher (MGL) is a non-lethal tool used to capture and ensnare targets. Using the MGL to control or tow a piloted vehicle requires a Hard difficulty Piloting (Planetary) check.
74-Z Speeder Bike: The 74-Z is among the oldest speeder bike models Aratech still produces. Vehicle Type/Model: Speeder Bike/74-Z. Manufacturer: Aratech Repulsor Company. Maximum Altitude: 25 meters. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 5. Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 3,000 credits/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Forward-mounted light repeating blaster—this weapon's entire profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 11; Critical 3; Range [Extreme]; Auto-fire, Pierce 1).
85-XS Odyssey Heavy Speeder Bike: Aratech's Odyssey is a speeder bike built for adventure.. Vehicle Type/Model: Speeder Bike/85-XS. Manufacturer: Aratech Repulsor Company. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 15. Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 4,500 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 4. Weapons: *None.
88-R \"Nightscreamer\" Speeder Bike: An exceptionally high-performance speeder bike manufactured by Ikas-Adno, the 88-R "Nightscreamer" is faster than some airspeeders.
8880 Luxury Landspeeder: Ubrikkian Industries' 8880-series luxury landspeeder delivers dignitaries in style and comfort. Vehicle Type/Model: Luxury landspeeder/8880. Manufacturer: Ubrikkian Industries. Maximum Altitude: 2 meters. Sensor Range: None. Crew: One chauffeur. Encumbrance Capacity: 30. Passenger Capacity: 6. Price/Rarity: 40,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *None. Built to Impress: All 8880 luxury landspeeders count as having a Luxury Passenger Compartments attachment installed, at no additional cost. This attachment cannot be removed, and does not count against the vehicle's total hard points.
9400 Grand Wanderer: The Ubrikkian Industries 9400 Grand Wanderer is more a mobile home than a vehicle. Vehicle Type/Model: Grand Wanderer landspeeder/9400. Manufacturer: Ubrikkian Industries. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 60. Passenger Capacity: 8. Price/Rarity: 35,000 credits/8. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *None. Smooth Ride: The 9400 Grand Wanderer, for all its lack of style, is a relatively comfortable accommodation, easily customizable to individual needs. A character who lives in one gains a boost die on any checks to recover strain while in the 9400.
A-11 Model III \"Burrower\": Designed to excavate mine shafts, the Model III Tunnel Boring Vehicle is a sophisticated machine capable of burrowing through virtually any material.
A-19 \"Iron Crawler\" Armored Transport: Following the loss of several mining facilities and digger crawlers in the Outer Rim Territories, the need to provide adequate defenses for its valuable assets on uncivilized, frontier worlds of the galaxy led by the Corellia Mining Corporation to manufacture a new armored transport with enough firepower to deter marauders and large, aggressive predators.
A-7 Hunter Interceptor: The A-7 Hunter Interceptor was Kuat Drive Yards' updated snub fighter entry into the inexpensive starfighter market in the years following the Clone Wars.
A-A3 Dray Light Speeder Truck: Smaller and lighter than the A-A5 speeder truck, the A-A3 Dray by Trast Heavy Transports is a common sight throughout the galaxy.
A-A4B Speeder Truck: Trast's big, square-shouldered A-A4B is an extremely popular short-haul cargo speeder typically used by small businesses for local deliveries.
A-A5m Emergency Response Vehicle: Developed based on the highly successful A-A5 speeder truck, the A-A5m is a purpose-built emergency response vehicle specifically designed to operate in harsh, inhospitable scenarios, whether unforgiving inclement weather or terrible disasters.
A-A8 BackCountry Speeder Truck: Another of Trast Heavy Transports' excellent speeder trucks, the A-A8 BackCountry is a purpose-built trekking vehicle marketed to explorers, scouts, and surveryors.
A-Type Stiletto: Nubia Star Drives makes some of the most distinctive vessels in the galaxy. The missiles are incredibly accurate and generally can kill another starfighter in a single shot. However, reloads must be purchased on Nubia and are expensive: each missile costs 1,000 credits and is (R).
A/SF-01 B-wing Heavy Fast Attack Starfig: The Alliance's B-wing heavy attack fighter is based on a prototype designed by the Mon Calamari engineer Quarrie. Hull Type/Class: Starfighter/A/SF-01 B-wing. Manufacturer: Slayn & Korpil. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: None. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Close. Ship's Complement: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 15. Passenger Capacity: 0. Consumables: One week. Price/Rarity: 150,000 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: *Hardpoint-mounted light ion cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Ion, Linked 2). *Hardpoint-mounted heavy laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]). *Forward-mounted proton torpedo launchers (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 8; Critical 2; Range [Short]; Blast 6, Breach 6, Guided 2, Limited Ammo 8, Linked 1, Slow-Firing 1).
AA-70 Observation and Command Speeder: The AA-70 is simply a medium-duty civilian speeder truck, typically the ubiquitous A-A5, modified for use by Alliance military personnel.
AA-9 Freighter-Liner: The AA-9 was designed as a massive bulk freighter, though it was quickly retrofitted into a low cost passen- ger liner. In the lead up to the Clone Wars, with the Separatist crisis on the horizon, these vessels were bought up by philanthropists to use as refugee trans- ports. Manufactured by a relatively obscure shipyard, the AA-9 popularised Botajef into galactic prominence with its iconic design. Despite a subpar hyperdrive, especially for a vessel that would be so widely used as a starliner, the AA-9 had a powerful trio of CEC Dyne 2577 radial sunlight engines. These massive thrusters gave the vessel a particularly high speed for its size. Its shape made it easy to land and dock in the starports across the galaxy. Commandos might be familiar with the vessel, as the GAR used the AA-9 as supplementary troop transports.
AAT-1 Hover Tank: Baktoid Armor Workshop's Armored Assault Tank Mk I, or AAT-1, is the CIS's primary infantry fighting vehicle. Vehicle Type/Model: Medium Repulsor Tank/AAT-1. Manufacturer: Baktoid Armor Workshop. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot, two gunners, one commander. Encumbrance Capacity: 10. Passenger Capacity: 6 on exterior. Price/Rarity: 75,000 credits (R)/6. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Turret-mounted heavy laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]). *One port and one starboard medium laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). *One port and one starboard heavy repeating blaster (this weapon uses personal scale) (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 15; Critical 2; Range [Long]; Auto-fire, Pierce 2, Vicious 1).
AO-2 Cometstrike-class Orbital Assault P: The AO-2 Cometstrike-class orbital assault pod fulfills a niche role among combat and landing craft, and its unique attributes are such that many people do not consider it a proper starship at all.
ARC-170 Heavy Starfighter: Incom Corp's Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 (ARC-170 to its pilots and crews) is the Republic Navy's premier heavy starfighter.
ARC-40b Scout-Reconnaissance Fighter: Kuat Drive Yards' ARC-40b is one of the unsung heroes of the Clone Wars. Hull Type/Class: Starfighter/ARC-40b. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 8. Navicomputer: None—astromech droid socket. Sensor Range: Medium. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot/sensor operator, one gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 10. Passenger Capacity: None. Consumables: Two months. Price/Rarity: 85,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Two forward-mounted light laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). *Two forward-mounted concussion missile launchers (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Blast 4, Breach 4, Guided 3, Limited Ammo 4, Linked 1, Slow-Firing 1). *Aft turret-mounted twin light blaster cannons (Fire Arc Aft, Port, Starboard; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Linked 1).
ARX-T3 \"Suncutter\" Light Interceptor: The ARX-T3 presents a very different approach to the starfighter compared to Koensayr Manufacturing's better-known Y-wing, with a focus on speed and maneuverability over armor or payload.
AT-CE Combat Excavator: The AT-CE combat excavator is another in Rothana Heavy Engineering's versatile walker series. Vehicle Type/Model: Walker/AT-CE. Manufacturer: Rothana Heavy Engineering. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot, one systems operator. Encumbrance Capacity: 15. Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 95,000 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: * Dorsal heavy repeating blaster—this weapon's entire profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc All; Damage 15; Critical 2; Range [Long]; Auto-fire, Pierce 2, Vicious 1). * Two hull-mounted light tractor beam emitters (Fire Arc Direction; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Tractor 2). * Beamdrill (Fire Arc All, Damage 10; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Breach 3, Inaccurate 3, Slow-Firing 1, Vicious 1).
AT-EST Exploration and Survey Walker: Developed by Rothana Heavy Engineering to serve the needs of the Old Republic's civilian exploratory corps, the AT-EST is a six-legged, low-slung walker designed to haul both passengers and cargo through rough terrain and hazardous environments.
AT-FRC Force Reconnaissance Walker: KDY's AT-FRC is a small, two-position walker designed for use by ARC troopers toward the end of the Clone Wars. Vehicle Type/Model: Walker/AT-FRC. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Medium. Crew: One drive/gunner, one reconnaissance systems operator. Encumbrance Capacity: 5. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 75,000 credits/8. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: *Forward-mounted auto-blaster (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 3; Critical 5; Range [Close]; Auto-fire). *Forward-mounted concussion grenade launcher—this weapon's profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 10; Critical 4; Range [Extreme]; Blast 8, Breach 1, Limited Ammo 20). Sound-Dampening Camouflage System: Thanks to its active and passive stealth systems, skill tests made to detect the AT-FRC upgrade the difficulty once.
AT-HCT: A derivative of the popular AT-EST, Rothana Heavy Engineering's All Terrain Heavy Civilian Transport has served the needs of isolated colonies for decades.
AT-ST Raider: Despite the New Republic’s best efforts many Imperial weapons have managed to escape the scrap yards. Some of the most valuable and dangerous pieces of equipment on the black market are AT-ST walkers. Run down, carbon scored, and in desperate need of an oil bath, these walkers have seen better days. However that doesn’t mean they’re any less dangerous. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
AT-TE: The lumbering, imposing All Terrain Tactical Enforcer is one of the GAR's premier fighting vehicles. Vehicle Type/Model: Walker or Heavy Assault Walker/AT-TE. Manufacturer: Rothana Heavy Engineering/Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot, one co-pilot/spotter, one vehicle commander, up to five gunners. Vehicle Complement: 2 AT-RT Recon Transport walkers. Encumbrance Capacity: 40. Passenger Capacity: Up to 20 troops with full combat gear. Price/Rarity: 100,000 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Dorsal turret-mounted mass driver cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 7; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Breach 2, Slow-firing 1). *Forward-mounted quad medium laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 3). *Aft-mounted twin medium laser cannons (Fire Arc Rear; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1).
AV-21 Landspeeder: A hot-rodder's dream ride, Mobquet's AV-21 is a limited-production landspeeder built for raw, straight-line speed rather than delicate handling.
AV-7 Anti-Vehicle Artillery Cannon: The primary artillery piece used by the Grand Army of the Republic, the AV-7 by Taim & Bak was a ubiquitous sight in all forward operating bases and ground campaigns, supporting the advance. It was specifically an anti-vehicle cannon, though its plasma ammunition was also particularly effective against the mechanical opponent it often faced, droids. It is crewed by only a single clone trooper, reducing the manpower needed to establish a full artillery battery. The weapon would deploy to the battlefield using a repulsorlift, as many hover tanks could, but would need to deploy its walker feet to stabilise for firing the main cannon. These walker legs could be used to move as well, to retain firing capacity. The cannon was similar to those mounted in the alcoves of a Venator-class star destroyer, with the damage output of a heavy turbolaser.
Acclamator-class Planetary Assault Ship: Rothana Heavy Engineering's Acclamator-class Planetary Assault Ship is a wonder of modern naval engineering. Hull Type/Class: Planetary Assault Ship/Acclamator-class. Manufacturer: Rothana Heavy Engineering. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 0.6, Backup: Class 10. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: 700 officers and enlisted crew. Starfighter Complement: None. Vehicle Complement: 320 speeder bikes, 66 LAAT/i, 14 LAAT/c, 48 AT-TE, 36 SPHA Walkers. Encumbrance Capacity: 10,000. Passenger Capacity: One GAR Legion—9,000 fighting troops, 6,000 HQ and support troops. Consumables: Six months. Price/Rarity: 110,000,000 credits (R)/8. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Three port and three starboard quad light turbolaser batteries (Fire Arc Port or Fire Arc Starboard; Damage 9; Critical 3; Range [Medium]; Breach 2, Linked 3, Slow-Firing 1).
Action IV Bulk Freighter: An ancient design, the Corellian Engineering Action IV has been a mainstay of the great hyperlanes for eons. Hull Type/Class: Bulk Transport/Action IV. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 3, Backup: None. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Medium. Ship's Complement: One pilot, 25 crew. Encumbrance Capacity: 8,500. Passenger Capacity: 6. Consumables: Six months. Price/Rarity: 150,000 credits/4. Customization Hard Points: 4. Weapons: *Two medium hull-mounted tractor beams (Fire Arc All; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Short]; Tractor 4).
Action VI Bulk Transport: The latest in the popular Action series of bulk transports, Corellian Engineering Corporation's Action VI is a prime example of what makes a transport.
Adz-class Destroyer: Once of the smallest capital ships in the Imperial Navy's arsenal, the Adz-class destroyer is a relative newcomer to the galactic spacelanes.
Aggressor Assault Fighter: Trilon Incorporated's Aggressor assault fighter blurs the line between patrol boat and starfighter. Hull Type/Class: Patrol Boat/Aggressor. Manufacturer: Trilon Inc. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 8. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot or One pilot, one co-pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 20. Passenger Capacity: 8. Consumables: Three months. Price/Rarity: 160,000 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *Forward-mounted twin medium laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). * Forward-mounted twin medium ion cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 4; Range [Short]; Ion, Linked 1). *Forward-mounted light tractor beam projector (Fire Arc Forward; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Tractor 2).
Ainik-class Scientific Survey Vessel: SoroSuub's Ainik-class is a small survey vessel designed for remote scientific missions. Hull Type/Class: Survey Vessel/Ainik-class. Manufacturer: SoroSuub. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 3, Backup: Class 12. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Extreme. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot, two engineers/scientists. Starfighter Complement: None. Vehicle Complement: Two speeder bikes or one landspeeder. Encumbrance Capacity: 150. Passenger Capacity: 6. Consumables: Six months. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *Dorsal and ventral turret-mounted quad laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Accurate 1, Linked 3). *Forward-mounted light tractor beam emitter (Fire Arc Forward; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Tractor 2).
Alabak's Gold: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Hull Type/Class: Light Freighter/Modified Wander-class. Manufacturer: Mon Calamari Shipyards. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 1, Backup: Class 10. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Medium. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot, one navigator, one engineer, two gunners. Encumbrance Capacity: 50 (without passengers). Passenger Capacity: 6. Consumables: One month. Price/Rarity: 83,700 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: (0 available). Weapons: * Forward-mounted twin laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]). * Dorsal turret-mounted twin laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]).
Alidade-class Long-Range Survey Ship: Once a common sight in the out-of-the-way places of the galaxy, the Alidade-class Long-Range Survey Ships are fast becoming a rarity in today's spacelanes.
All Terrain Anti Aircraft: One of the less well-known members of Rothana Heavy Engineering's AT-series walker family, the AT-AA was first produced during the final years of the Clone Wars.
All-Terrain MegaCaliber-6 Heavy Assault: Despite their similarities in stature, the AT-M6 is a radical departure in function from the smaller AT-AT, the M6 is a field artillery piece more akin to the venerable SPHA-T walker from the Clone Wars than it is to any sort of troop transport. The walker was effectively built around its primary armament, the MegaCaliber Six turbolaser, a warship-grade weapon mounted on a ground platform a fraction of the size. The frame of the walker is hardened to the highest standards thanks to advances in metallurgy allowing lighter materials to match the performance of older armor, and layering these plates for even greater protection, even deflecting glancing blows. The unique posture of the AT-M6 has given it the nickname “Gorilla Walker” due to the more heavily fortified forelegs, all the better to support the weapon that gives the walker its purpose.
All Terrain Armored Cargo Transport: The All Terrain Armored Cargo Transport is a heavy-duty logistics walker and cargo hauler based on the successful All Terrain Armored Transport..
All Terrain Armored Transport: Part troop carrier, part heavy assault vehicle, and part terror weapon, the Imperial Army's All Terrain Armored Transport is one of the heaviest vehicles on the modern battlefield.
All Terrain Attack Pod: The AT-AP is a dedicated light field artillery walker used extensively by the Grand Army of the Republic. Vehicle Type/Model: Walker/AT-AP. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Medium. Crew: One pilot, two gunners. Encumbrance Capacity: 12. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 90,000 credits (R)/5 or 100,000 credits (R)/6. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Forward-mounted heavy mass driver—this weapon uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 8; Critical 3; Range [Extreme]; Breach 4, Slow-Firing 1) or forward-mounted heavy mass driver (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 8; Critical 3; Range [Extreme]; Breach 4, Slow-Firing 1). *Dorsal turret-mounted medium laser cannon (Fire Arc Direction; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]).
All Terrain Defense Pod: Kuat Drive Yards' All Terrain Defense Pod is a light, fast, bipedal patrol walker recently introduced into the Imperial Army.
All Terrain Defense Turret: The AT-DT is a self-propelled artillery piece fielded by the Imperial Army. Vehicle Type/Model: Artillery Walker/AT-DT. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot/gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 10. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 44,000 credits (R)/7. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: *Forward-mounted self-loading artillery cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Medium]; Blast 3, Breach 2, Inaccurate 2, Slow-Firing 1, Vicious 2). *Forward-mounted twin light blaster cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Linked 1). Long-Range Support: Upgrade the difficulty of ranged combat checks using the AT-DT's artillery cannon once if the target is within medium range in personal scale.
All Terrain Exploration Transport: A smaller variant of Rothana Heavy Engineering's AT-EST Exploration and Survey Walker, the All Terrain Exploration Transport (AT-ET) walker is still being used to traverse new worlds throughout the galaxy.
All Terrain Open Transport: The All Terrain Open Transport is the Republic’s troop carrier of choice for traversing difficult terrains where aerial transport is inadvisable. The AT-OT is reminiscent of the AT-TE, with an additional pair of legs, two missing frontal guns and no mass driver cannon. Instead of a cannon, the hull is an open structure in which the clones can be carried across hazardous terrain. When seated, passengers are protected from ground attack due to the height of the armour and walker’s clearance. However, the open top was a massive weak spot which could easily be exploited by aircraft. The AT-OT was used extensively on swampy planets with thick forest cover, reducing its weakness against air attack and serving the purpose of troop transport where other vehicles would otherwise struggle.
All Terrain Personal Transport: Developed by the Republic during the Clone Wars, the AT-PT is a small, one person infantry walker designed to carry a single trooper into battle in even the most hostile environments.
All Terrain Reconnaissance Transport: The AT-RT is a small, lightweight, one-seat walker first fielded by the Grand Army of the Republic during the Clone Wars for reconnaissance, forward control, and escort and light strike duties.
All Terrain Riot Control Transport: First introduced in 19 BBY, the AT-RCT is a small, two-man, open-topped walker used by Imperial police forces as a crowd control and anti-riot vehicle.
All Terrain Scout Transport: The Imperial Army's AT-ST is a medium-sized bipedal walker designed as a fast force reconnaissance vehicle. Vehicle Type/Model: Reconnaissance Walker/AT-ST. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot, one gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 18. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 75,000 credits (R)/5 or 50,000 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: *Port-mounted light blaster cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Close]). *Starboard-mounted concussion grenade launcher—this weapon's entire profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 10; Critical 4; Range [Long]; Blast 8, Breach 1, Limited Ammo 15). *Chin-mounted twin heavy blaster cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 4; Range [Close]; Linked 1). or weapons: *Port-mounted light blaster cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Close]).
Alliance T-47 Sandspeeder: The Arda Base mechanics have customized a total of eight T-47 airspeeders to better fly through the surrounding canyons, dubbing them "sandspeeders."
Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing Starfighter: The Alpha-3 Nimbus, known colloquially as the V-wing is the Republic Navy's newest starfighter. Hull Type/Class: Starfighter/Alpha-3 Nimbus. Manufacturer: Kuat Systems Engineering. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None—astromech droid socket. Sensor Range: Close. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one astromech droid. Encumbrance Capacity: 5. Passenger Capacity: 0. Consumables: One day. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits (R) or 70,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 2. They also include overhead space for luggage storage. The shuttle is driven by a single pilot who is seated in an elevated glass cockpit in the front of the ship. Entry and exit from the vehicle is facilitated by use of a staircase droid, which is deployed in ferry stations. The BD-T is usually equipped with a screen above the door displaying information about the shuttle's destination, and overhead lights, as well as wide windows which offer a clear view of the surrounding locales.

Built in orbital shipyards, ships of the class were designed to never enter a planet’s atmosphere, instead they were docked at space stations and transfer yards to load and unload cargo. Baudo-class Star Yacht: Instantly recognizable thanks to their sleek curves and triangular profile, Baudo-class star yachts are among the most exclusive vessels in the galaxy.

Two variable sets on its quad barrel exhausts help it perform both at low or higher speeds. Able to handle high temperatures and taking advantage of an impressive boost module, the Quadrijet 904E can generate great speed out of its engines on demand, but the lack of cooling will be felt after a couple of uses. Agility was definitely an afterthought during its conception though, and it is prone to a lot of skid. The Quad Barrel is also known to be difficult to control for racers unfamiliar with the commands and the pilot interface, but will prove surprisingly responsive otherwise. The ship has clearly been designed from the keel up as a pure, single- purpose warfare machine. Composed as one, large solid wedge shape, the Brute is powered by a pair of oversized ionisation reactor engines giving it significant speed. Bristling with turbo lasers, quad laser turrets, and concussion missile launchers, these ships are dedicated starfighter killers.

Caisson-class Engineering Troop Carrier: A survivor of the Clone Wars, the Caisson-class is a heavy dropship designed to carry combat engineers and their equipment into action.
Cal Perquo: A truly ancient design, the Cal-class (otherwise known as the Kumauri-class) dates back to the earliest days of the Republic. Before effective planetary shields, its asteroid accelerators were key weapons for planetary bombardment. The sturdy design of the Cal-class has enabled them to last long beyond their expiry dates. The Cal Perquo has been a pirate vessel for generations, menacing under-developed planets and isolated mining stations with its vast mass driver cannon. Civilian Airspeeder: Designed to carry passengers, cargo, or some combination of both, civilian airspeeders are ubiquitous throughout the galaxy.
Class A Modular Transport: Manufactured by Kaniff Yards, and used primarily by smaller Outer Rim trading and transport companies, the Class A Modular Transport a well-rounded workhorse. It’s not unusual to see Class As As that have been in service for well over a century. A massive, lumbering vessel, it carries 20,000 tons of bulk cargo. in its cavernous cargo hold. The ship itself is as utilitarian as a ship can be. A small module containing the bridge, crew quarters and primary systems sits off to the side of the vessel. A massive, lumbering ship, it carries 20,000 tons of bulk cargo. in its cavernous cargo hold. The ship itself is as utilitarian as a ship can be. A small module containing the bridge, crew quarters and primary systems sits off to the side of the vessel, connected by a secured access tunnel. The remainder of the ship is given over to cargo space.
CloakShape Fighter: The KSE CloakShape is an aerospace fighter first built by the now-defunct Kuat Systems Engineering in the decades leading up to the Clone Wars.
Collor Pondrat Plug-2 Behemoth: This model of podracer definitely stays in the line of large, intimidating factory-custom engines made by its manufacturer. Unlike its rather experimental predecessor - the Plug-F Mammoth - however, this one favors a more balanced approach with a very good overall performance, solid controls on top of a powerful boost. The large radiators mounted in a radial shape at the back of the engines offer a stellar ability at sustaining heat in spite of the decidedly abysmal cooling package coming with it, which stays pretty much in line with Collor Pondrat’s philosophy on designs able to shrug off heat so well that they don’t need much cooling in the first place. The engines also tend to be on the more fragile side for their size and their gigantic air intakes feed into a blocky combustion chamber, giving onto four exhausts on each engine and offering great propulsive power in spite of its mass.

It trades boost and efficient cooling for more raw performances in speed and maneuverability, also at the cost of finicky commands and inputs and a relatively unstable fuel mixture. The sheer size of these ramjet engines and their angular, menacing design make it instantly recognizable, all without sacrificing air intakes. Cordair Limousine Speeder: The Cordair Limousine Speeder is a luxury, personal airspeeder. Perma-polished quadranium steel is moulded into beautiful flowing curves and decorated with silver highlights. Polished intake radiators sit on the speeder’s two front vanes in a neo- Industrial style. The Cordair’s two-seater passenger compartment is appointed in plush nerf leather and lamas wood-grain. Discretely hidden buttons allow for a privacy screen between the passengers and the speeder’s single driver. Currently available in “Cobalt Burn”, “Rose Scarlet” and “Polished Onyx”, the Cordair is a favourite among Imperial elites, with the trend, like all trends, starting on Coruscant. Prominent Senators such as Mon Mothma, Orn Free Taa and Gila Thule all travel the capital in style in their Cordairs. Despite the Cordair’s sleek and elegant design, the speeder is surprisingly robust under the hood.

Modular Cockpit: As an action, the pilot may disconnect the cockpit, abandoning the rest of the vessel. The craft's silhouette becomes 2 and it s encumbrance capacity becomes 10. Anything in the back of the ship (potentially including systems and attachments) is left behind.
Corellia Mining Corp. Digger Crawler: Built by the Corellian Mining Corporation as massive mobile mining and smelting facilities, digger crawlers appear to be treaded boxes that towers some twenty meters above the surrounding landscape.
Crusader Corvette: The Crusader is a fast picket boat whose bold, angular design bristles with point-defence weaponry. Though the sharp lines of the craft evoke battleships of the Mandalorian crusades of ages past, the protective nature of the design better fits with the New Mandalorian philosophy of pacifism and defence. With oversized engines and highly sophisticated software, the Crusader is designed to rush to the defence of ships threatened by starfighters; this makes it an ideal patrol boat, defending freighters from encroaching pirates, but just as capable as a support player in a major fleet battle. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Curich-class Shuttle: Sienar Fleet Systems is best known for production of TIE-series starfighters and other military craft for the Empire, but they do not design only warships.
Customized Firespray System Patrol Craft: The Firespray-class system patrol craft was designed by Kuat Systems Engineering at the end of the Clone Wars. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Cutlass-9 Patrol Fighter: Produced in vast quantities by the SoroSuub Corporation prior to the rise of the Empire, these fighters were intended for use by SoroSuub alone.
Cybershop Ship: Cybershop ships are mobile cybernetics showroom, installation, and maintenance facilities. Hull Type/Class: Corvette/Modified CR90. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 15. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: 135 officers and enlisted crew, including medical staff. Encumbrance Capacity: 1,000. Passenger Capacity: 100 passengers, 80 patients, 40 medical droids. Consumables: One year. Price/Rarity: 1,300,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 6. Weapons: *One dorsal and one ventral turret-mounted twin medium turbolaser battery (Fire Arc All; Damage 10; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Breach 3, Linked 1, Slow-Firing 1).
D-4 Mobile Defense Platform: Incom developed this heavy landspeeder as a heavy response defense vehicle for urban crisis zones, and many militaries have adopted it for rescue operations.
D-4s Projection Platform: After the success of the D-4, Incom realized that the large generator necessary for the shield system could be used to power other devices.
D-type Stealth Freighter: Nubian vessels tend to be built to unusual specifications, and few are more unusual than the D-type. Hull Type/Class: Light Freighter/D-type Stealth Freighter. Manufacturer: Nubia Star Drives, Inc. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 3, Backup: Class 25. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one system engineer. Encumbrance Capacity: 125. Passenger Capacity: 3. Consumables: Two months. Price/Rarity: 200,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 0. Weapons: *None. The D-type benefits from state-of-the-art stealth systems that render it almost invisible to passive and active scanners. The ship will not register on passive scanners that are short range or further away from it (only passive scanners at close range can pick it up). In addition, increase the difficulty of any checks made to detect the vessel by 2.
DP20 Gunship: Known colloquially as the "Corellian Gunship," the DP20 is designed from keel up as a pure, single-purpose military vessel.
DS-1 Death Star: In all galactic history, no construct has ever matched the size, awesomeness, terror, or destructive power of the Death Star.
DS-2 Death Star: Soon after the destruction of the first Death Star, the Emperor secretly ordered construction of a second, more powerful battle station.
Daimlo Engineering Banshee-11: Originally produced for daimlo pilot Zanales, this model of podracer sports strong air scoops not dissimilar to the Radon-Ulzer models at the front of the engines, which increases maneuverability and cornering dramatically. The materials used in its making are sturdy and the frame robust, but this comes at the cost of repair efficiency and acceleration. Fortunately, the cooling system coming with it ensures that heat is only an afterthought, for otherwise relatively standard low-pass turbofan engines. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Darvro-class Light Freighter: Along with the Tallanx fighter, the Darvro is a ship built on stealth and speed commonly used by the Ord Gimmel Defense Fleet.

Defoliator Deployment Tank: The DDT was a platform for a new experimental weapon which used the AAT hull as its basis. The new weapon, the defoliator capsule launcher, replaced the original turret. In addition, stabilisers were extended out to the rear of the tank’s base to account for the heavier main weapon. Only two prototypes of this tank were ever reported to Republic Intelligence, and both were destroyed in action. However, it is entirely possible that the DDT would enter limited production in the later years of the war. Unlike the AAT, the DDT was more artillery than tank. The launcher needed to be loaded manually by droids outside the tank. While it could be close to the front lines, it needed to be properly protected at all times. The defoliator was a fearsome piece of technology to face as a clone or other biological being.
Deliverance: The Galactic Standard medical symbols painted on the clean white hull of the Deliverance seem somewhat incongruous with the sweeping wings and blunt, turbolaser-studded prow of the ship. An old but proven design, the Deliverance has served in corporate fleets for decades. Since its acquisition by Athakam Medtech, it has been refitted with advanced on-board medical facilities so it can deliver emergency medical care in the most hostile of situations. Despite this, however, it mostly serves to protect the corporation's valuable assets- whether that be from piracy or defaulting clients... This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Delta-12 Skysprite: The Delta-12 Skysprite-class is a rare two-seat version of the Delta-7 Aethersprite light interceptor. Hull Type/Class: Starfighter/D-12. Manufacturer: Kuat Systems Engineering. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None—astromech droid socket. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 8. Passenger Capacity: 1. Consumables: Two weeks. Price/Rarity: 85,000 credits/8. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *None. Designed by TransGalMeg in association with KSE, the Syliure-31 is a medium-range hyperspace sled made to provide hyperspace capabilities to the Delta-7, Delta-7B, and Delta-12 starfighters. The Syliure-31 is a simple affair consisting of two powerful ion engines and a medium-range Class 1 hyperdrive giving the Delta-series fighters respectable range and hyperspace capabilities.
Delta-6 System Defense Starfighter: Small, fast, and agile, the Delta-6 Sprite-class starfighter was introduced by Kuat Systems Engineering in the decades leading up to the Clone Wars.
Delta-7 Aethersprite-class Interceptor: Small, fast, and deadly, Kuat Systems Engineering's Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor is one of the newest additions to the Republic's vast arsenal.
Delta-class T-3c Shuttle: The Sienar Delta-class T-3c is a heavily armed command shuttle used by high-ranking Imperial officers and other valued officials.
Dewback 2 Repulsor Sled: The Dewback 2 is a basic cargo-hauler repulsor sled made by Aratech Repulsor Company. Vehicle Type/Model: Repulsor sled/Dewback 2. Manufacturer: Aratech Repulsor Company. Maximum Altitude: 5 meters. Sensor Range: None. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 30. Passenger Capacity: 4. Price/Rarity: 4,000 credits/2. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *None.
Diamond-class Cruiser: The Diamond-class was colloquially known as the Commerce Guild cruiser due to its wide use along guild tradelines. Produced by the very guild, it was designed as a hyperspace patrol cruiser and given an experi- mental hyperdrive capable of traveling 30% faster than even the top military drives. This allowed it to respond faster than pirates may expect. In addition, it had a great sublight speed for pursuit of pirate vessels. Standard armament was lacking, but its massive size was more than enough to intimidate vessels into fleeing or submitting. The Diamond-class was also capable of landing on planetary surfaces unaided and unloading a mass of guild droids to rapidly establish order and compliance.
Dikutruni: Kad Solus named his ship Dikutruni, which translates roughly as "soul of the fool." Hull Type/Class: Shuttle/modified Aja'jor. Manufacturer: MandalMotors. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: None. Navicomputer: None—astromech droid socket. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 75. Dreadnought-class Heavy Cruiser: Although considered old and outdated at the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Rendili StarDrive's Dreadnought-class heavy cruiser is still regarded as one of the more capable vessels of its class and size in use today.
Droch-class Boarding Ship: A common sight aboard CIS warships, the Droch-class boarding ship is one of many boarding and utility ships used by the CIS Navy.
Droid Tri-Fighter: A recent addition to the CIS navy's inventory, the droid tri-fighter is one of the best aerospace superiority fighters in the galaxy.
Dual Hemisphere-Omni Support Vessel: A smaller version of the Lucrehulk-class by the same manufacturer, the DH-O support vessel was intended as a slow commercial freight prior to the Clone Wars. In comparison to the larger vessel, this one was dedicated to freight and not expected to be used in any combat scenario. As such, it was reliant on shielding and armour to survive piracy. With marginally less cargo capacity than a Lucrehulk, the slower hyperdrive was not of great concern. During the Clone Wars, the DH-O was not widely seen until later in the conflict. It was pressed into service beyond the role of a supply transport, and into a support role during blockades and planetary defence or invasion. In some cases, it was used as a secondary command and control vessel, due to its greater hardiness than the Lucrehulk. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
E-7 E-Wing Multi-Role Starfighter: Produced by FreiTek, a recently formed offshoot of Incom, the E-wing multi-role fighter is the newest starfighter in the Rebellion's arsenal.
EF-97 Intelligence Cruiser: The EF-97 Intelligence Cruisers operated by the Imperial Security Bureau are an insidious threat to freedom of thought across the galaxy. Fitted with high-resolution sensor arrays and powerful computer systems, they lurk in the darkness of inhabited systems, sifting through the communications of entire planets for signs of seditious or disloyal activity. An EF-97 does not announce its presence as a Star Destroyer would; in many cases by the time rebels or political dissidents in-system realise the eyes of the Empire are upon them, and the ISB's trap is ready to be sprung. To prevent intelligence leaks, the EF-97 has a small sentient crew augmented by a large contingent of droids who are routinely memory-wiped. A small number of Imperial Security Bureau analysts oversee a bank of tactical droids who tirelessly sift through the data.
EF76 Nebulon-B Frigate: Kuat Drive Yards' Nebulon-B frigate was one of the first capital ships deployed to Imperial fleets, coming into service alongside the first Imperial-class Star Destroyers.
Elsinore-Cordova Turbodyne 99-U: Known for its highly unusual, insectoid profile, the Turbodyne 99-U’s main strength lies in its maneuverability and its ability to sustain high engine strain. With its lighter, shorter engines, the design is also focused on mastery over speed variations rather than pure optimal speed, boasting good acceleration on top of advanced airbrakes, and the pod behind offers unparalleled control and avionics. Otherwise unremarkable, it still allows for an unusually high service ceiling with powerful repulsors, probably tied to its need for a higher clearance than the average podracer. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Enser Flay 254: This short podracer with unusual vertical and flat air intakes was built specifically with marine species in mind: indeed, the cockpit is enclosed and hermetically sealed so that it can host water, or any liquid necessary for certain species to pilot the vehicle without dying. This specific design can also come in handy for subaquatic species that can breathe in normal atmospheres, but still perform better in their natural environment. As a result, the podracer is relatively fragile and the enclosed pod especially vulnerable, which is why it comes equipped with a powerful repair suite. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Eta-2 Actis-class Light Interceptor: Kuat System Engineering's Eta-2 Actis is a small, fast, lightweight fighter descended from KSE's elderly Delta-series fighters.
Eta-class Shuttle: First released before the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Cygnus Spaceworks' Eta-class shuttle is a study in stylish utility.
Eta-class Supply Barge: The Empire rewarded the Telgorn Corporation with a major Imperial Navy contract for logistics modernization after Telgorn's presentation of the Eta-class supply barge and Zeta-class shuttle as a complete system of logistics vessel.

Vehicle Complement: 200 assault shuttles, transport shuttles, and support craft, 24 AT-ATs, 50 AT-STs, 2 prefabricated garrison bases. Encumbrance Capacity: 105,000. Passenger Capacity: 38,000 troops and support staff. Consumables: 6 years. Price/Rarity: 325,000,000,000 credits/9. Customization Hard Points: 2.
Exelbrok XL 5115: This is a simple model of podracer, which is nonetheless capable of reaching solid speeds and boasts a decently layered performance, where nothing is truly lacking. Its wide, double-layered turbofan powerful engines are capable of handling big speed variations and sustain its boost quite effectively, although their durability leaves something to be desired, which its designers seem to have had in mind when accomodating for its repair system. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Experimental CIS Super Tank: This tank entered its first round of production during the Second Battle of Geonosis and their factory became the primary target of a Repblic offensive following the first wave of the invasion. Despite their first deployment in battle being thwarted by a cunning trap laid by the legendary Anakin Skywalker, the plans may have survived and the tanks later deployed during the Clone Wars final years. Similar in silhouette and shape to the MTT by the same manufacturer, Baktoid Amour Workshop, this heavily armoured combat speeder was also protected by ray shields. This made it virtually untouchable, even by the most powerful artillery units. In addition to this, it was heavily armed with a suite of weapon batteries to deploy the most appropriate response in every foreseeable situation. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Extended Range Z-95 Headhunter: The highly popular, if ageing, Headhunter series of starfighters has variants for every occasion. The Extended Range edition was originally intended for use as a scout ship, though after decades of reliable use they have been phased out of most militaries. Their capacity for independent operation has helped most find new homes on the second hand market. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
FG 8T8-T: Perhaps the most unique and visually striking Podracer currently on the market, the 8T8 is a technological wonder in the world of Podracing.
Far*Reach Reconnaissance Ship: Ships from Loronar's Far*Reach line of scout ships are among the most popular short-range scout/reconnaissance vessels in the galaxy.
Farwan & Glott FG 8T8-Twin Block2 Specia: The 8T8's design is unusual in that the cockpit is joined to the fanjet engines forward of their intakes via fixed, fully faired brackets. With the Twin Block2 Special, the need for traditional podracer devices is eliminated, making the craft bear more of a resemblance to a traditional airspeeder. However, this configuration also puts the pilot at greater risk of injury or death from head-on collisions and prevents a good view and control by having most of the vehicle located behind instead of forward. Because of this threat, the pod puts heavy emphasis on turning, acceleration and braking power over speed, allowing great control. The cockpit is aerodynamic and fully enclosed, including a factory-equipped ejection system rare in factory customs and luxury lines. Despite this, the model is among the slowest podracers on the market due to its weight, size, and overbearing pilot assistance.

FireStar II-class Orbital Defense Statio: The FireStar II–class battle station was designed to provide concentrated firepower from a smaller, less expensive, and more easily produced station the original FireStar.
Firespray System Patrol Craft: With its distinctive upright flying position and rotating internal cockpit that allows for horizontal landings, the Firespray was once considered evolutionary in both looks and design.
Fishing Trawler: From the estuary moon of Trask to the idyllic lakes of Naboo, fishing trawlers are a common sight on any planet with a body of water larger than a puddle. These vessels are designed to sustain month long voyages, though most fishermen prefer to come into port every night. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Flare-S Swoop: Popular among mercenaries, swoop gangs, bounty hunters, and adrenaline junkies throughout the galaxy, the Flare-S is the very epitome of the swoop builder's art.
Flasherfish Personal Watercraft: Based upon the popular Undicur-class jumpspeeder, the Flasherfish was designed specifically to fill the personal transportation niche on water planets where the local meteorological conditions precluded standard flyers and swoops.
Flatline and Brilliance: The Flatline and the Brilliance are both Interceptor IV-class frigates. Hull Type/Class: Frigate/Interceptor IV. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Fondor Haulcraft: A popular mid-range ship, the Fondor Haulcraft has developed a reputation as an extreme solid ship and is commonly seen in the hands of traders and travellers looking for the reliability and precision associated with the Fondor name. Perpetually ranked third behind Corellia and Kuat in starship construction, Fondor has staked its reputation not on mass-production but instead on quality. Many of the best designed frigates and capital ships are built at the shipyards there, from the Super Star Destroyer Executor to luxury Hutt yachts. The Haulcraft was Fondor’s attempt to enter the smaller, freighter market, a market dominated by Corellia’s YT series of starships. Unable to compete with the YT series’ low price or high modularity, the Fondor Haulcraft instead emphasised reliability and build quality, two aspects that are often complained about in YT ships.
Foot Speeder: These curious contraptions combine the swiftness of a speeder car with an easy of storage and simple construction that makes keeping and maintaining them much less expensive than doing so for an actual vehicle.
G-1A Heavy Starfighter: The G-1A heavy starfighter is a large, sturdily built craft with a flexible battlefield role that endears it to some Outer Rim freelancers.
G35 \"Silver Cloud\" Airspeeder: A midsized airspeeder designed for corporate and light military use, the G35 is popular among business executives and moneyed interests of the Corellian Sector.
G9 Rigger Light Freighter: Already considered an antique at the outset of the Clone Wars, Corellian Engineering Corporation's G9 Rigger-class light freighter is a rare sight in the modern spacelanes.
GAT-12H Skipray Blastboat: Another pre-Clone Wars design, Sienar Fleet Systems' Skipray Blastboat is a heavily armed and armored patrol craft and gunboat descended from the ancient Turbostorm-class gunships used by the Old Republic.
GPE-3130: Galactic Power Engineering's 3130-series factory custom is one of the stranger Podracers currently on the market. Vehicle Type/Model: Podracer/GPE-3130. Manufacturer: Galactic Power Engineering. Maximum Altitude: 50 meters. Sensor Range: Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 1. Passenger Capacity: 0. Price/Rarity: 11,500 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *None.
GR-75 Medium Transport: Inexpensive and reliable, Gallofree Yards' GR-75 medium transport is one of the most common bulk freighters in the spacelanes.
GS-100 Salvage Ship: The GS-100 salvage ship, more commonly known as a Trandoshan scavanger ship, is a Gallofree Yards vessel, designed to navigate debris fields in the wake of space battles, picking up salvageable parts to sell for scrap. A durable vessel, capable of dealing with dangerous space flotsam, it requires a small crew to operate, which allows the owner to split the profit from salvage with associates.
GX1 Short Hauler: Originally designed by Lantillian ShipWrights as an affordable yet luxurious pleasure craft, the GX1 has remained a staple of the spacelanes despite an ever-shifting market.
Galactic Power Engineering GPE-3130: The GPE-3130 is a model of podracer designed for straight-line speed performance, but favoring a good weight to thrust ratio instead of using oversized engines for raw power. Its high speed under boost combined with a good baseline maneuverability makes it a terror on raceways with broad, gentle curves, but otherwise difficult to control in tight spaces or sharp corners. Its twin ThrustMaster 9000 aerospike rocket engines, augmented by a pair of smaller ramjet engines are also known to require seemingly endless maintenance and the fuel needed to power them is highly volatile and prone to exploding. The vehicle is awkward-looking and its repulsorpod is uncomfortable at best, with an unintuitive control layout, which makes the craft universally panned by critics. This is a homebrew adjustment of an existing vehicle for use with a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gaze Electric: A truly one of a kind vessel, the Gaze Electric is the flagship of Marchion Ro, Eye of the Nihil and commander of its legions. The ship was built almost 150 years ago as a mobile home and temple for the Path of the Open Hand cult. An extremist missionary order originally from the frontier world of Dalna, the Path believed that the Force should be free and never be used by anyone, including the Jedi Order. At some point, the Path’s mobile temple fell into the hands of the Ro family and they took the strange ship as their own flag ship. More haunted fortress than starship, the Gaze Electric has a distinctive profile with a narrow midship section flanked by a bulbous and jagged module forward and a flat arrowhead section aft. Running all along the top of the ship are sensor aerials, spires and towers, giving the upper decks the look of crumbling castle parapets.

Ghtroc 720 Light Freighter: An ambitious, but ultimately failed attempt to compete with CEC's YT-series, Ghtroc Industries wagered heavily on the 720 freighter.
Ginivex-class Fanblade Starfighter: This unique starfighter was designed by the Geonosian Huppla Pasa Tisc Shipwrights Collective. Another example in the use of a solar sail, it was capable of greater speed and shield. The downside to this was making the starfighter give off a greater signal on sensor arrays. These were largely made to commission, making them particularly rare; especially beyond the Clone Wars era. While the crimson energy sail provides this vessel with exceptional dogfighting potential in space, it must be stowed when entering the atmosphere and when preparing to go to hyperspace. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gladiator–class Star Destroyer: Originally conceived by the legendary Walex Blissex as a Star Destroyer escort in the heavy battle lines of the Clone Wars, the Gladiator was repurposed by the Galactic Empire as an independent, long-range patrol craft.
Golan I Space Defense Platform: A heavily armed and armored space platform, the Golan I is the first in a series of planetary space defense platforms built by Golan Arms.
Gozanti-class Armed Transport: The CEC/Gallofree Yards Gozanti-class armed transport is a cruiser-sized vessel used by numerous private and governmental organizations as a bulk cargo carrier and convoy escort.
Groundcar: The groundcar is as primitive as it is ubiquitous on poor or recently colonized worlds. Vehicle Type/Model: Groundcar/Various. Manufacturer: Various manufacturers. Sensor Range: Close. HA-5 Aerial Retreat: More mobile dwelling than vehicle, the HA-5 aerial retreat is an unusual repulsorcraft, resembling nothing so much as a square, three-story building lifted into the sky.

HLAF-500 Starfighter: A modified and upgraded version of the LAF-250 starfighter, the Heavy/Light Attack Fighter 500 boasts a number of significant improvements over its predecessor.

HSP-10 Pursuit Airspeeder: Once a common sight in the skies of Coruscant and other Core Worlds, the HSP-10 pursuit airspeeder is being phased out in favor of newer models, though it is still widely used by Imperial and local planetary security forces beyond the Inner Rim.
HT-2200 Medium Freighter: Solidly built and packed with state-of-the-art avionics and cargo-handling systems, the massive, slab-sided HT-2200 medium freighter is a relatively new ship design from Corellian Engineering Corporation (CEC).
HWK-290 Light Freighter: Designed decades before the Battle of Naboo, the HWK-290 light freighter was Corellian Engineering Corporation's first attempt to break into the rapidly expanding "executive light freighter" market.
Hammerhead Corvette: Rendili StarDrive's Sphyrna-class corvette, more commonly called the "Hammerhead corvette," is a common sight in the galaxy's spacelanes.

Harbor Hauler: Upon taking power one of the New Republic’s first acts was to regulate Kuat Drive Yard’s production of Imperial-era war machines. With their All Terrain line of assault walkers effectively outlawed, KDY turned its attention to more commercial vehicles. Within a year they were the galaxy’s leading manufacturer of mobile cargo haulers. The All Terrain Harbor Hauler (AT-2H) can effortlessly traverse a variety of terrains, including water up to 15 meters in depth. Similar to ascension guns, the crane combines high tension Steelton cables and winches with integrated repulsors to carry loads up to three times the walker’s weight. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Hardcell-class Interstellar Transport: The Techno Union’s own bulk transport vessel, the Hardcell-class was an ageing craft by the time of the Clone Wars. Originally filling the role of bulk transport for Techno Union droids and with a secondary capacity as a refuelling station, it was one of the first vessels to see battle in the Clone Wars. Unfortunately, it was relatively ineffective due to its glaring weak points and lacking military ability. The weapons were only intended for deterrence of piracy, not fighting in a war. Despite featuring a military grade hyperdrive, the rest of the ship systems were ancient. It used rocket boosters as its main engines, giving it awful maneuverability and even worse atmospheric performance. These boosters enabled the capital class vessel to make planetary landings unassisted, and gave a significant speed boost to the ship (once it was able to get back off the ground).

ILH-KK Citadel-class Light Freighter: Produced by Koensayr, a company known more for its starfighters and starships components than for cargo ships, the Citadel-class is a small, relatively fast freighter built to compete with CEC's YT and YV class of light freighters.
IML-2c Aquatic Scout Ship: Initially designed and produced to work in conjunction with larger base ships, the aquatic scout ships were designed to scout out possible mining locations.
IR-3F-class Light Frigate: The predecessor to Republic Sienar Systems' IPV-1-class corvette, the IR-3F-class light frigate is an old and outdated patrol craft first introduced during the later decades of the Old Republic.
Imp. Juggernaut: The Imperial Combat Assault Transport, sometimes referred to as a Juggernaut (Not to be confused with the HAVw A6 Juggernaut) is a heavy cargo vehicle. Riding on massive wheels, it is a compromise between AT walkers and repulsor craft. At the height of the empire the unarmed vehicle would have been escorted by repulsor tanks and TIE fighters. With most of their combat vehicles destroyed in the war these transports must rely now on their armor and speed for protection. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Impeding Assault Tank: The IAT was an exotic anti-infantry walker designed by the Umbaran Ghost Armaments corporation. It was controlled by a single crew member and was covered in over a dozen blaster cannons. The pilot could use its weapon systems in a targeted way, especially its fire-linked and articulated forward-facing blaster cannons, or use its dorsal turrets as an area of denial weapon to suppress masses of infantry. Its name comes from the role it took in battlefields, where it would tunnel into the middle of enemy formations and emerge with a blanket of blaster fire. As with many of Ghost Armaments’ products, the IAT was not widely available for sale, used exclusively by the Umbaran forces to deter invasions of their planet. It has cutting edge technology, using the same reflex control interface found in all their vehicles.
Imperial Customs Corvette: Whilst the Imperial Star Destroyer is the imposing face of Imperial oppression, many traders and smugglers have far greater fear of the humble Imperial Customs Corvette. A fast, relatively maneuverable vessel with a bevy of tractor beams and boarding clamps, the Corvette exists to patrol the spaceways of inhabited systems, ensuring compliance with the petty regulations and swingeing fees of the Imperial customs regime. The Customs Corvette has a bridge rising high above a forked body, designed to catch and clamp smaller freighters between the arms, in order to allow the stormtrooper squads it carries to board the vessel and frisk it for contraband. Though highly effective in this role, and capable of shaking off attacks from the occasional pirate or Rebel starfighter, the Customs Corvette is not a combat vessel; in high-risk systems it is commonly accompanied by Customs Frigates.
Imperial Customs Frigate: Though designated as a frigate, Imperial Customs employs this ship as a large patrol boat to police the space lanes and starports of the Empire.
Imperial Dropship Transport: The Imperial Dropship Transport bears more than a slight resemblance to the Low Altitude Assault Transport, although its smaller and more angular build is distinctly in the modern Imperial style.
Imperial Floating Fortress: Designed as an urban counterpart to the AT-AT walker, the Floating Fortress is better at navigating the narrow streets and sharp corners of dense cities.
Imperial II–class Star Destroyer: More casually known as the "ImpStar Deuce" by the Rebels who fly against it, the Imperial II–class Star Destroyer is the next-generation successor to the Imperial I–class.
Imperial I–class Star Destroyer: Considered the "face of the Imperial Navy," and one of the most powerful ships in current service, KDY's Imperial–class Star Destroyer is part of a long line of frighteningly powerful battleships fielded by the Imperial Navy.
Imperial Light Infantry Speeder: Usually deployed to worlds without much of an Imperial presence, the Light Infantry Speeder is intended to be a versatile all-round vehicle that can perform well in a variety of situations. The four-seater landspeeder is manned by one pilot, and can hold three other passengers , one of which can take control of the forward-mounted WD-90 repeating blaster cannon. This gunner usually rides in the prow, scanning for targets. The mounting attachment gives the gunner a wide, lateral field of fire. The blaster cannon can also decompress or elevate to defend against aerial and ground threats and can even be removed completely and placed in the prow, when the speeder is not on patrol duty. The LIF is an ugly, boxy, wedge shaped design, the much smaller and cheaper brother of the Occupier Assault Tank.

Infantry Support Platform: Known colloquially as the Republic’s swamp speeder, the ISP was designed as a heavier version of the BARC speeder. The design lines are reminiscent of the bike’s unique shapes, certainly showing Aratech’s influence in the production of this weapon platform. While it was shipped standard with light blasters equipped to its frontal mounts, these weapon systems were often swapped out for more appropriate ordnance for its support role in the particular battle. While the BARC speeder bike is good for conducting recon, the additional armour and weapons on board the ISP allows for patrol duties to be conducted in greater safety for the clones. Beyond patrol duties, the ISP was particularly effective against vehicles which it was able to outmaneuver by skimming along watery and uneven surfaces at high speeds. Irateq RQ 550C Dart: The RQ 550C Dart is designed around a philosophy of precision and pure performances over any other consideration. With absolutely no room for weight, most of its plating and systems are made from incredibly lightweight materials, at the cost of sturdiness, leaving in fact, no room for mistakes either. All margins have been cut and pushed to their absolute limits in order to get a podracer with unmatched maneuverability, controls and support systems. However, the frame, on top of proving extremely fragile and brittle, cannot support the strongest engines, which are also very prone to overheating, somewhat balanced by an impressive cooling system. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

This ensures that the unaerodynamic pod remains stable during flight, and is also known to shield the pilot in the case of catastrophic events leading the pod to collide with the ground, rolling it to safety instead. The pod blocking all view ahead means that it comes equipped with a screen linked to an external camera for the pilot. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

J-1 Semi-Autonomous Proton Cannon: The primary artillery piece used across the Clone Wars by the CIS, the J-1 came with a pre-installed droid brain capable of following basic orders from a command unit nearby. However, it also had an operator seat from which the cannon could be fired and moved. Unlike its counterpart, the AV-7, this cannon was particularly more powerful but required a crew to reload the canister after each shot. A group of J-1 proton cannons could alone defend an emplacement from aerial assault, capable of ripping through a dropship with a single round, or even penetrating an Acclamator-class assault ship’s shields. It was however, slow and lumbering, making it particularly easy to avoid. In addition, the cannons were susceptible to ground attacks as the powerful shell was too dangerous to fire at short range. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
J-type Diplomatic Barge: The J-type vessels produced by Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated began as a collaboration with the Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps serving the royalty and government of the planet Naboo.
J-type Star Skiff: A sleek, delta-wing-shaped vessel with a distinctive teardrop fuselage, the J-type Star Skiff was designed for the Royal House of Naboo.
JAK J930 Dash-8: With a solid maneuverability and an emphasis on integrated systems, and sizeable fins acting as radiators for an outstanding cooling system making up for medium sized jet engines prone to overheat, this podracer is designed for performance and agility before toughness: it is in fact fragile, its fins pretty much doubling engine size, but very also nimble and offers a solid speed. Able to accelerate very well at will, the J930 Dash-8 is perfectly suited to face any unexpected scenario, at the cost of durability. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

A JT-12C jetpack allows a person to function as a silhouette 1, speed 2, handling 0, system strain threshold 4 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere and requires Piloting (Planetary) to operate.
Jadthu-class Landing Ship: Possibly one of the most heavily armored troop carriers ever developed, the Jadthu-class landing ship was once the premier lander of the Grand Army of the Republic.

Jedi Sojourner: During the final years of the Old Republic, the Jedi Order commissioned Kuat Systems Engineering (designers of the Delta-7 Aethersprite interceptor, fielded in large numbers by the Jedi) to provide them with a ship designed to carry Jedi Knights and their Padawans on long journeys of meditation and training.
Jedi Training Cruiser Crucible: The Crucible was a legendary ship that served the Jedi Order as a mobile training center and classroom for nearly a millennium.
Jedi Vector: The Jedi Order’s main starfighter during the High Republic era is Valkeri Enterprise’s Jedi Vector. Designed to be a physical extension of the Jedi's connection to the Force, the Vector is a sleek and streamlined vessel that emphasises performance and manoeuvrability. The Vectors were designed from a minimalist perspective. They lack systems that would otherwise be essential to non-Force-sensitive pilots, including shielding and important computer components. The ships are designed to transmit every tactile response directly to its Jedi pilot. Although this fluid responsiveness allows pilots to pull off complex manoeuvres, it also comes at the price of requiring a great deal of focus from its operator. Despite the Vector's spindly appearance, its firepower is not to be ignored.
Jerserra's Reaper Tactical Assault Speed: Jerserra's favored transport vehicle is her customized airspeeder from Sienar Fleet Systems. Vehicle Type/Model: Airspeeder/Reaper TAS. Manufacturer: Sienar Fleet Systems. Maximum Altitude: 200 kilometers. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot, one gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 8. Passenger Capacity: 4. Price/Rarity: 27,500 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Forward-mounted light repeating blaster (this weapon's profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale; Fire Arc Forward; Damage 11; Critical 3; Range [Extreme]; Auto-fire, Pierce 1).
Jet Pack: A jet pack is ideal for fast maneuverability and surprise attacks. A jet pack allows a person to function as a Silhouette 1, Speed 2, Handling 0, System Strain Threshold 3 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere, and require Piloting (Planetary) to operate.
Joben T-85 Speeder Bike: The T-85 Joben is a sporting and enthusiast's speeder bike produced by the Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation, a small speeder manufacturing company located in the Outer Rim.
JumpMaster 5000: Corellian Engineering's JumpMaster long-range scout is a small vessel designed for autonomous exploration and reconnaissance.
K79-S80 Imperial Troop Transport: The Imperial Troop Transport, sometimes called the Recon Troop Transport, is a large, heavy-duty landspeeder used to move stormtroopers, prisoners, and supplies between planetside locations.
KSE Rho-1 Limulus-class Courier: Kuat Systems Engineering's Limulus-class was a common courier ship used by the Jedi during the final years of the Old Republic.
KST-100 Kestrel Light Executive Transpor: Corellian Engineering Corporation's KST-100 Kestrel light executive transport is yet another largely failed attempt by CEC to gain entrance into the competitive VIP transport market.
KV 9T9-B Wasp: Keizar-Volvec, a producer of speeder and starship systems headquartered on the Outer Rim world of Temako, has a reputation for producing well-made, sturdy, and woefully bland products that perform well but are otherwise unexciting.
KV Swoop: Perhaps the most common personal transport vehicle in the Outer Rim, the ubiquitous KV by Kuat Vehicles is the purest expression of the swoop concept available today.
Kalevalan Star Yacht: Officially designated the Nau'ur-class, "illumination" in Mando'a, these yachts are commonly referred to simply as Kalevalans as the custom-order nature of their production precludes the commonalities of a typical ship class.
Kaminoan Observation Ship: First developed by the cloners on Kamino to maintain oversight of their charges during training and deployment, these large ships were purpose-built to provide a mobile platform from which detailed, scientific observations could be made under a variety of circumstances.

Weapons: *Dorsal turret-mounted quad laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Accurate 1, Linked 3).
Keelkana-class Attack Submersible: Mon Calamari Subworks created a number of distinct submarines, and the Keelkana-class attack submersible is one of its greatest triumphs.
Keizar-Volvec/ Voltec KV 9T9-B Wasp: Produced by the typically unimaginative engineers at Keizar-Volvec, the 9T9-B does not defy expectations in its blandness. The first Podracer produced by the company, the Wasp is competent and well-designed, if not aggressively average. Using a pair of KVT Series 400 fanjets, the control system for the pod is sophisticated and durable, allowing the Podracer to take full advantage of their ample thrust power albeit bad brakes. Despite its staidness, the 9T9-B is highly stable due to its efficiently designed exhaust cones, and reliable overall, a rare quality in a Podracer. Easily maintained and modified, its hull is modular in design which makes it a prime candidate for racers who enjoy tinkering. This is a homebrew adjustment of an existing vehicle for use with a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Keldabe-class Battleship: The Keldabe-class battleship became known for its construction and deployment by the criminal Zann Consortium, after they stole the design from MandalMotors.
