Koensayr AD-1S Modular Multi-Role Starfi: As one of the galaxy's major starship parts suppliers, Koensayr seeks to maximize profits through the combination of parts expertise and supply distribution system.
Kom'rk-class \"Gauntlet\" Fighter: The Kom'rk-class fighter was the work of rogue members of MandalMotors corporation working for the Mandalorian terrorist group Death Watch.
Krayt Fang - Customized YT-1300 Light Fr: This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use. Weapons: * One Dorsal and One Ventral Turret Mounted Medium Laser Cannon (Skill: Gunnery; Range Close; Damage 6; Critical 3).
Kulless Squall: The Kulless Squall is an unique podracer of custom design created by the former podracer mechanic Shrivel Braittrand, using materials coming entirely from a refurbished Bin Gassi Quadrijet, and radically redesigning the engines by removing the quad barrel for a single main exhaust centered around a jet cone. While it took away the sheer speed performances of the late Bin-Gassi, it still retains a solid capacity in that field, and gains what its progenitor lacked: some turning and maneuverability. While unable to keep most of the heavy plating built around the engines of the Quadrijet, the Kulless Squall still proves surprisingly tough for a podracer that shed most of its shell. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Kurtob KRC 410C: Sporting retro-styled asymmetrical engine housings, this podracer has been outclassed after its discontinuation and displays very lax performance compared to other racing machines. With its short fanjets, The KRT is mediocre in every way, but if the machine has one potentially redeeming factor, it is its Boost threshold granted by its enormous chambers dedicated to post combustion, which more than doubles the design's design's intended limits. This means that the 410C can potentially boast a maximum velocity approaching 820kph, but this is not advisable, as performing at that speed can quickly overheat the engines. Speeds over 1000 kph have been recorded utilizing the KRT-410C's overdrive "design flaw", but this was achieved only after intense and very expensive modifications. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

LAAT/le Patrol Gunship: Part of the powerful LAAT series of military gunships, the LAAT/le is a slimmed-down, demilitarized model built for use by law enforcement in dense urban areas.

Live-Haul manufactured by Rothana Heavy Engineering was the default choice for the transport of massive live cargo by the Republic's Department of Agriculture and Nonsentient Creatures at the time the Clone Wars began.

Using the LRTS requires the operate to make an Average difficulty Computers check. Maps produced with the LRTS add an automatic success result to any Survival checks made to navigate using the map plus an additional success result for every two advantage results generated by the Computers check.

LR1K Sonic Cannon: One of the iconic weapons used at the outset of the Clone Wars during the Battle of Geonosis, the LR1K is a particular specimen of Geonosian engineering. Manufactured by Gordarl Weaponsmiths, the sonic cannon was a vehicle without its own method of propulsion. It did have an integrated repulsor engine but required a beast of burden such as the Orray to pull it along. Primarily used as a crowd control weapon, with the same technology as used in the Geonosian sonic blaster, it could easily kill a group of clones or jedi with a single blast. The cannon required two crew members to operate, though a targeting computer system did most of the heavy lifting in its use. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Lambda-class T-4a Long Range Shuttle: The T-4a, or Lambda shuttle, as it's more well-known, is a commonly encounter utility shuttle in service with the Imperial Navy.
Lancer-class Frigate: The Imperial Navy's Lancer-class frigate, a dedicated anti-starfighter vessel with an impressive reputation for power and reliability, has been in service since the end of the Clone Wars.
Lancer-class Pursuit Craft: Borrowing liberally from Corellian Engineering Corporation's design language and philosophy, the Lancer-class rides the line between civilian freighter and warship.

Now, they serve the Galactic Empire as oppressors themselves. Helmed by officers in dead-end career postings, they enforce petty Imperial directives with vigour of the bored and resentful. Longbeam Cruiser: One of the Galactic Republic’s newest light cruisers, the Longbeam is already making waves across the galaxy. Designed at the Republic Shipyards on Hosnian Prime, the Longbeam is the brainchild of shipwright, Joors Tankar. The ship fills a need in the Republic, that of a cruiser armed enough to protect itself while on missions, but not too armed as to constitute a purely military vessel. The ship’s profile resembles that of a Glee Anselm hammerfish, with a blunt forward skull, tapering behind into a long tailfin. The Longbeam can be handily crewed by only three, and a single pilot can control many of the ship’s functions alone, in an emergency. The rest of the ship’s interior space is divided between well appointed state rooms, medical facilities, storage bays and a modular unit. This modular unit can be removed and swapped for mission specific loadouts.

M12-L Kimogila Heavy Starfighter: A long-range, multipurpose starfighter more durable than most craft used by Hutt kajidics, the Kimogila is the product of a contract between the Hutts and MandalMotors to design and build a ship to meet the kajidics' needs.
M3-A Scyk Interceptor: A small fleet interceptor manufactured by MandalMotors, the M3-A is named after a swift, vicious lizard native to Tatooine.
MC-18 Light Freighter: Mon Calamari Shipyards is one of the few manufacturers in the galaxy openly supporting the Rebellion, and many of the most advanced Rebel ships are produced by the skilled artisans working there.

MC75 Star Cruiser: The MC75 was the first of the many Mon Calamari city-ships that went through the conversion from civilian use to military operations.
MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser: Long and sleek with a decidedly organic silhouette, these elegant vessels are among the few heavy lines of battleships possessed by the Rebel Alliance.
MC80a Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser: Similar in size, strength, and role to the Imperial Navy's Star Destroyers, the Mon Calamari Home One type star cruiser is an imposing vessel.
MEV2 \"Dug Digger\": The Mining Excavation Vehicle, nicknamed the "Dug Digger" for a perceived resemblance to the aliens known as Dugs, has seen extensive service by the Corellia Mining Corporation.
MG-100 StarFortress SF-17: The MG-100 StarFortress SF-17, also known as the B/SF-17 heavy bomber, is a model of heavy bomber manufactured by Slayn & Korpil for use by the New Republic during the late stages of the Galactic Civil War. Following the Galactic Concordance and New Republic disarmament, the ship has primarily used in civilian applications. Several ships were used by the Resistance during its war with the First Order around 34 ABY, earning it the name Resistance Bomber. The modular bombing magazine, called the “clip” by the bomber’s crew, would drop proton bombs through sequenced electromagnetic plates in the clip, which propelled the bombs to “drop” in microgravity environments. The bombs would then be drawn magnetically to their targets. The assembly could be programmed to drop specific sections of the payload in sequence, but the most common configuration was “deploy all.
MPO-1400 Purgill-class Star Cruiser: The Purgill-class was named after the massive spacefaring whale-like creatures that were capable of natural travel through hyperspace. The class actually contained a variety of vessel profiles, but the MPO-1400 was specifically designed by the engineer known as Shug Drabor, using their particular thirteen pod engine formation. This cruiser was quite large, capable of holding a few hundred passengers in relative luxury while visiting the vistas of the galaxy, especially the edges of the Unknown Regions. In service since the romantic and peaceful era of the High Republic, the longevity of this vessel is a testament to its quality of build and fitness for purpose. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
MSP100 Pteropter Hover Pod: Based off the design originally used by Trandoshans in their hunts on their homeworld and surrounding colonies, the Pteropter is perfectly suited for short exploratory expeditions.
Maelstrom-class Battle Cruiser: The Maelstrom-class battle cruiser belongs to the family of dagger-shaped proto–Star Destroyers, such as the Venator and Acclamator classes, used by the Republic Navy at the outset of the Clone Wars.
MandalMotors M22-T Krayt Gunship: Commissioned by the notorious crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure, these impressively armed gunships were parceled out to his favored lieutenants as rewards for loyal service and to help them keep any wayward elements of his organization in line.
Mandalorian Protectorate \"Fang\" Starfi: The designers of the Mandalorian Protectorate starfighter, or "Fang" fighter, consider it to be the pinnacle of space superiority vehicles.
Mankvim-814 Light Interceptor: Hastily designed and assembled by a Pau’an subsidiary of the Techno Union, the Mankvim-814 was a stubby starfighter with an oversized ion drive. The engineers at the Techno Union had to make a number of additions to the original hull so that the interceptor could effectively dogfight, installing repulsors and additional deployable winglets for atmospheric operations. As it would be deployed from carrier groups and operate as planetary defence craft, atmospheric performance could not be overlooked. The resulting starfighter was an affordable interceptor that could be mass produced by the Techno Union’s self-replicating factories. Due to the number of these produced, they could be found cheap following the Clone Wars, and many were acquired to protect the Corporate Sector Authority systems on the Outer Rim.
Manta RamAir Mark IV Flat-Twin Turbojet: This massive podracer boasts powerful twin ramjet reactors linked into one, bringing it up there with some of the best models in terms of raw power and straight line performance. But like for a lot of super-heavy machines it takes time for the engines to reach their full potential, engines that are well respected for their resilience before seeing their integrity threatened. It is however poorly maneuverable, and the support systems are average to passable. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Marauder-class Assault Corvette: The reliable Marauder-class corvette is tried-and true, having seen service in navies the galaxy over since the days of the Galactic Republic.
Mauler Tactical Responder: Sienar Fleet Systems' Mauler airspeeder is a fast, lightweight troop carrier and gunship marketed to law enforcement agencies and private security companies.
Max-7 Rono Freighter: The ultimate example of form over function, the Max- 7 Rono from SoroSuub is one of the simplest cargo freighters on the market, with its boxy design basically unchanged for well over a century now. Originally built for internal use by SoroSuub, the Rono consists of two sections; the first is a large hexagonal shaped hold section, the sides of which open outward and can be used as loading ramps. The second section of the ship is the elevated, front- facing command cockpit, accessible via a ladder from the hold section. In the rear of the ship are its six engines—three on each side—which include both sublight and hyperdrive units. All other extras and amenities have been stripped from the freighter.
Millennium Falcon: Certainly, the most famous YT-1300 in existence, the Millennium Falcon is the ship that made the Kessel Run in less than twelve parsecs, the ship that spirited the Death Star plans to the Rebels' Massassi base, and the ship crewed by Heroes of Yavin.
Mining Guild Ore Crawler: The Mining Guild often rolls out a large landspeeder known as an ore crawler to planets intended for full exploitation of resources. They are capable of clearing landscapes of all biological life to allow for direct access to surface deposits of ore and raw materials. They were once restricted to uninhabited planets, cleared for use by Republic license, but the Empire has relaxed such regulations. They are slow and bulky, but particularly well armored for a civilian vehicle. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Minstrel-class Space Yacht: Custom-made by Ubrikkian Industries for the Hutts, these large luxury space yachts rival many corvettes and smaller warships for size and power.
Mist Hunter: Named for the ammonia cloud vapors on Gand, the Mist Hunter is a G-1A owned and flown by the Gand bounty hunter Zuckuss and his droid partner, 4-LOM.

Mobile Tac-Pod: Rothana Heavy Engineering’s Mobile Tactical Pod is a troop transport and dropship used by security forces across the galaxy. Famed for their engineering masterpiece that was the Republic’s Low Altitude Assault Transport (LAAT) during the Clone Wars, Rothana wanted to adapt the principles of that ship but on a much smaller scale, the Mobile Tac-Pod was the result. The small dropship is capable of ferrying up to 10 passengers to planetary engagements in its cramped, padded interior. A single pilot in an elevated cockpkit controls the ship as well as its weapons systems. The passengers can enter and exit the Pod through a rear entry ramp, which is accessible when the Pod’s wings are retracted into landing mode. The Tac-Pods are armed with a single laser cannon placed in a ball mount on the chin, as well as a fixed cannon located on each of the wing root -just outside of the engine cowling.

OG-9 Homing Spider Droid: A model of self-propelled artillery manufactured by the Baktoid Armour Workshop and widely used by the Commerce Guild, the OG-9 was an entirely independent unit on the battlefield. Their role was to enforce Guild laws and perform asset protection on the wilderness ridden planets where many of their exploits are located. Unfortunately for this model, they were often employed in the role of regular armed walkers, something the manufacturer had not accounted for. While the spider droid is capable of traversing all sorts of terrains, this was with the intent of positioning the artillery in prime positions which would be advantageous for providing cover fire. The legs aren’t well armoured, and the main mass of the droid contains an over-clocked reactor core which is prone to violent explosions when the casing is ruptured.
Octuptarra Magna Tri-droid: The culmination of the insectoid and odd designs of the Techno Union, the octuptarra magna tri-droid is an upscaled version of the already large octuptarra droid. Designed for all terrain combat, this droid is a true artillery walker and is also sufficiently armoured to fight close to the front lines. As per its name, it carries a trio of weapons upon a rotating disk at the peak of three legs. These weapons will swap out during an advancing assault, allowing the droid to make multiple attacks with its laser cannons in quick succession. Additionally, the ordnance pods run alongside these cannons, and they can also fire in quick succession while they reload in a different facing position. The octuptarra was deployed in similar battlefield formations to the OG-9, but was better suited in the role of frontline artillery.

The hulls built from the strong wood of the wroshyr trees, the craft was originally designed for sports and fishing. As such, this vehicle was not particularly apt for combat—though many were pressed into service during the Battle of Kashyyyk. Thanks to their civilian nature, they were quite adaptive and easy to retrofit. By the end of the clone wars, the catamarans had been retrofitted with missile launchers, bomb chutes, and repeating blasters. However, it was one of the more dagger-like designs, with a profile that was more reminiscent of Sienar or KDY. Armed relatively lightly, with a pair of frontal guns and a rear-facing turret, it was largely designated an attack shuttle due to its armoured frame, shields, and speed. One Man Submersible Devilfish: The OMS Devilfish was produced for the Grand Army of the Republic SCUBA troopers for use in theaters located on water worlds. Conceptually similar to a STAP or speeder bike, the Devilfish doesn’t have a compartment for its pilot. Instead, the user holds onto the control bars and is tugged along by the submarine. While the Devilfish is rated for a depth of 350 metres, it is simple enough to function much deeper, as long as the user is also capable of surviving at those depths. The on-board weapons could make quick work of most CIS droid models deployed underwater, and also fight off larger submarine craft. However, it did not provide much cover for the user, who could easily be shot off the submarine. This meant that the Devilfish often survived engagements and was regularly salvaged by the opposition to use for themselves.
Ord Pedrovia: A custom podracer fully designed by professional pilot Gasgano, the Ord Pedrovia boasts a nice speed boost package helped by a gigantic combustion chamber and four side boosters affixed to it, although the engines turn very hot after boosting. Its wide exhaust cones also help funneling the airflow output while keeping the exhausts thin. The whole machine has been so finely tuned to its limits that only the best pilots can take advantage of its full potential. Sporting a camera built in the back of the pod directly connected to a holoscreen on the control panel, it is possible for the pilot to keep an eye on a fully comprehensive three dimensional footage of any competitor tailing the vehicle during a race. Visually identical to the iconic TIE/ln starfighter, the Outland TIE features folding solar collection panels and a landing gear. These enable it to land without a standard TIE docking armature and grants easy access for pilots. These added mechanism add mass to the fighter, making it slightly more sluggish than a standard TIE. Equipped with powerful Arcex IX ion engines, and a lightning quick Mahara nav system the Nuna is capable of astonishingly straight- ahead speed and aerial agility from low altitude to the limits of the atmosphere. A large pair of Zordaine ion engines sit at the aft of Pacifier, ensuring the cruiser maintains a solid cruising speed. The cruisers usuallymount a pair of Taim & Bak twin turbolasers in dorsal and ventral midship turrets as their main weapon. RZ-2 A-wing: The RZ-2 A-wing interceptor, also known as the RZ-2 A-wing starfighter or the Resistance A-wing, was a wedge-shaped starfighter manufactured by Kuat Systems Engineering after the success of the former RZ-1 A-wing interceptor used by the Alliance during the Galactic Civil War. The RZ-2 incorporated and standardized years of RZ-1 field modifications by the Rebellion, along with making the chassis slimmer and longer to yield even more speed. As such, piloting the ultra-fast yet temperamental starfighter was a matter of pride for various Resistance pilots regarding their skills and daring. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Raddaugh Gnasp Fluttercraft: Another example of wookiee engineering, this ornithopter is a particularly exotic form of airspeeder. Named after the predatory insects found on the Wookiee colony moon of Alaris Prime, this scouting craft was often used by rescue and reconnaissance operations through the jungles of Kashyyyk. As such, developed originally for civil security, the fluttercraft was eventually pressed into service during the Clone Wars for quick transportation of couriers and recon units. It had to rely on its nimble and rapid frame to avoid being hit by enemy fire, but it was fitted with a rear-mounted laser cannon so that it could deter opponents from pursuit while escaping. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Radon-Ulzer 620C: This podracer became instantly famous when the human racer Anakin Skywalker won the Boonta Eve Classic circa 32 BBY. Heavily customized and modified by its pilot, it earned the title of fastest podracer in the galaxy. However, this is the standard version, which doesn’t shine by its speed performance, but rather its highly adaptable profile, solid controls and top notch maneuverability. Not among the toughest podracers podracers on the market, its engines can still pack a punch for their size when compared to the competition on the smaller end. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Raider-class Corvette: Designed six years before the Battle of Yavin to counter starfighters and other attack craft, the Raider-class corvette was panned by Imperial strategists as an ineffective frontline warship.
Raider II-class Corvette: Designed six years before the Battle of Yavin to counter starfighters and other attack craft, the Raider-class corvette was panned by Imperial strategists as an ineffective frontline warship.
Razor Crest: Military patrol craft that predate the Empire, Razor Crest series gunships are renowned for their rugged reliability. Compared to contemporary starships their on-board systems are highly simplified, making repairs easy. The ship comes standard with a two medium laser cannons, but there is room to install more robust weapon systems. The interior is also highly customizable, with room for weapon lockers, crew quarters, recreation terminals, food prep areas, or just simple cargo storage. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Regulator-class Patrol Vessel: Sienar Fleet Systems' Regulator-class patrol vessels have been a staple of intersystem law enforcement across the galaxy for decades.
Rendili StarDrive Neutron Star–class Bul: Rendili StarDrive's Neutron Star–class bulk cruiser was once a popular with planetary defense forces throughout the galaxy.

Repulsor Pack: While lacking the range and power of a true jetpack, a repulsor pack provides a reliable—and less terrifying—alternative for those new to personal flight devices.
Retrofitted AT-AT: Never sparing any expense when it comes to his own protection, Moff Dardano has acquired the Armored Fist, a decommissioned and only slightly damaged All Terrain Armored Transport (or AT-AT, in more common parlance) for his assault on the base. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Rocket Boots: The ability to take flight without warning can provide a combatant with a huge advantage over an opponent. Rocket boots enable a person to function as a silhouette 1, speed 1, handling –3, system strain threshold 4 or 3 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere and requires Piloting (Planetary) to operate. At the end of each round of use, rocket boots suffer 1 system strain as their fuel depletes.
Rogue-class Starfighter: The Rogue-class starfighter is an example of the effect that CIS cost cutting can have on an artisinal starfighter model from a well established manufacturer. Taking the Porax-38 as a base model, the engineers at the Baktoid Armour Workshop sought to create a copy which could be produced more widely and assigned to elite units such as the MagnaGuards. In the process, each change they made to reduce costs was matched by another change which made it just as expensive once again. All the bells and whistles of the Porax-38 were left aside, including its enhanced sensors and commonly fitted Class 1 hyperdrive. This freed up the budget to upgrade the weaponry of the Porax-38 to heavy laser cannons. The enhanced weaponry left the craft only marginally cheaper than the original model. With the stripped systems, the Rogue-class was more modifiable than the original craft.
Rothana Experimental Walker: The Rothana Experimental Walker (abbreviated REW) is the latest and greatest large personnel walker developed by Rothana Heavy Engineering. The REW is as yet unnamed and only a handful of prototypes have been produced, including one for the Republic Fair on Valo. The REW is a six- legged, low slung walker designed to hold both passengers and cargo though rough terrain and hazardous environments. A number of significant mining companies have already placed orders for the REW, for when it enters full production. In addition to being incredibly sturdy, the REW is designed to be very user friendly, with a sophisticated ED-N droid brain managing the majority of the walker’s operations. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Rufescent Cargo Hauler: A widely produced bipedal heavy loader, TaggeCo.'s Rufescent-class cargo hauler has achieved popularity due to its modular nature and ease of modification.

SCS-19 \"Sentinel\" Armored Landspeeder: A transport intended to convey valuable items or important individuals, the SCS-19 "Sentinel" is among the most heavily armored personal vehicles available on the civilian market.
SS-54 Light Assault Gunship: Designed to compete with the likes of the GAT-12 Skipray and the Firespray-31-class patrol craft, the SS-54 is a small, relatively fast vessel built for short-range patrols and ground attack.
STAP Assault Platform: Descended from the cheap, ubiquitous civilian airhook, the STAP is a lightweight, single-position repulsorlift craft used extensively by the CIS military.
SU-40 High-Performance Airspeeder: Where the AV-21 is among the most highly sought after high-performance landspeeders in the galaxy, SoroSuub's SU-40 is its airborne counterpart.
SU-42RS Flashblind Aerial Reconnaissance: The SU-42RS Flashblind, a militarized version of SoroSuub's SU-40 high-performance airspeeder, is built for high-altitude penetration and aerial reconnaissance.
Sabine's TIE Fighter: Sabine considers the vibrant paint job she gave to a TIE fighter the Spectres stole to be perhaps her finest work. Hull Type/Class: Starfighter/TIE series. Manufacturer: Sienar Fleet Systems. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None. Sensor Range: Close. Ship's Complement: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 4. Passenger Capacity: 1. Consumables: Two days. Price/Rarity: 60,000 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Forward-mounted medium laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). EMP Blast: Sabine has been known to rig her TIE fighter with a multitude of stolen EMP grenades, enough to disable even a Star Destroyer. Activating the grenades must be done via remote detonation (as it would be extremely unhealthy to be in the cockpit when they go off).
Savrip: The oldest ship in the Sorority fleet, the Savrip, a Marauder-class assault corvette, was the pirate band's original flagship. [...] the Savrip is equipped with an electronic countermeasures suite.
Scavenger Tug: Heavy salvage jobs require specialized tools for the trade. When derelict bulk freighters cannot be repaired or towed into dock for a complete overhaul, or when capital ships meet catastrophic ends, they must be dismantled and disposed of. Usually based from another ship or space dock, a salvage crew is often dispatched in a Scavenger Tug, equipped with fine manipulator arms, and plasma cutting torches. Once the wreckage has been separated into smaller pieces, the tug uses its tractor beam projector to haul them to a collection ship or cargo container.
Scoop Skimmer: Produced by the relatively unknown Utapau-based vehicle and starship manufacturer Buuper Torsckil Abbey Devices, the scoop skimmer is a civilian speeder which has gone beyond filling the sinkholes of Utapau and found itself used across many core planets of the galaxy. A relatively standard civilian model, the repulsor was more reliable due to the tighter regulations and standards found on Utapau. The reliability is sought after by a market which doesn’t expect to get into fights with their speeders, hence how quickly the vehicle spread across the galaxy. On Utapau, a special model of the scoop skimmer is often seen in the local authority’s livery and mounting emergency lights. While not particularly impressive as a pursuit craft, it saved the local governing body from spending money in the importing of specialised police vehicles.

Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 3,000 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 11; Critical 3; Range [Extreme]; Auto-fire, Pierce 1).
Scurrg H-6 Bomber: Developed several years before the Clone Wars, the Scurrg is a powerful all-purpose combination starfighter/bomber, and one ahead of its time. Originally offered to Mid-Rim worlds like Naboo as a flexible solution for patrolling their spacelanes in the face of unrest in the Outer Rim, sales faltered in the face of the apparent peace. Though war arrived soon after, many of the few Scurrgs produced had already been stolen or sold on the black market, and the high per-ship price and unproven experimental bomblet generator prevented its adoption by the nascent Republic military. Still, as a robust and flexible ship the Scurrg is in great demand in the age of the Empire, in particular amongst pirate bands for whom the energy bomblet generator is far more useful than expensive traditional ordnance.

Secutor-class Battlecarrier: Perhaps the largest dedicated starfighter carrier in the Imperial arsenal, the massive Secutor-class battlecarrier is an old design from the earliest days of the Imperial Navy.
Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery Turbolase: A particularly massive walker produced in the same vein as the others made by Rothana Heavy Engineering, the SPHA-T was a mobile weapons platform with a built-in full personnel deck for operating and directing the main weapon system. This system was a modular one, capable of being swapped out for other specialised weapons for use on the battlefield. When used by the Republic, the special turbolaser mounting was used. This weapon was capable of outputting multiple pulses worth the fire of a battery of turbolasers within a short period of time. Unlike most artillery units, however, its construction prevented it from firing indirectly, needing a direct line of fire to its target. However, its huge size was a major impediment to its role as ‘mobile’ artillery.
Sentinel-class Landing Craft: A product of the same Sienar Fleet Systems/Cygnus Spaceworks collaboration that produced the Lambda-class T-4a shuttle, the Sentinel class is the Imperial Navy's primary assault ship and landing craft.

Serabyss Howler 1165: The Serabyss Howler is a cumbersome podracer that in the fashion of the Plug-G Behemoth, hits surprising degrees of agility unheard of for vehicles of that size. Its straight turbofan engines boast a solid speed but prove unable to accelerate well due to their size, which indicates that more consideration was brought to other aspects of the craft. Unparalleled controls and cooling however complement its boost package, allowing for repeated uses of the latter even considering its otherwise average performances. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Shadow Caster: This highly modified pursuit craft was intentionally designed to provide planetary security for worlds that couldn't afford to maintain their own starfighter squadrons or to purchase extravagant capital ships with which to defend themselves.
Shadow Raptor: Originally the Imperial escort frigate Eccentric Lad, the Shadow Raptor was liberated from Contruum Shipyard by Airen Cracken and his crew of resistance fighters when he fled his homeworld.
Sheathipede-class Shuttle: The Sheathipede-class shuttle, known colloquially as the Neimoidian shuttle, is the CIS navy's premier light lifter and VIP transport.
Shelba 730 Razor: The Shelba 730, once adorned with proper livery, is often considered as one of the most flashy and elegant podracers in tournaments. What its medium rocket engines lack in the speed department, they make up for with a potent thrust and an elaborate, efficient control system. Average elsewhere overall, it also comes with unusually short steelton cables tying the pod to the engines. The long fins affixed at the front of the flat intake of each engine can be used as air flaps, much like the ones attached at the back around the exhausts. A series of lateral radiators also help the podracers shedding excess heat and cool the engines but at the expense of aerodynamics. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Slightly Rusted AT-ST Walker: The Empire often deploys these light war walkers in harsh terrain. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Solemn Star: The Solemn Star is an ageing Jedi Sojourner retrofitted with ion weapons given its purpose as a transport for padawans. In this purpose, it makes for a fine home away from home for the Jedi on board. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Star Galleon Armed Transport: Though used to provide armed escort for the Perlemian convoy, the two Star Galleons are also used to transport cargo when necessary.
Starbolt Assault Carrier: Corellian Engineering Corporation's Starbolt assault carrier was sold as an integrated ship and weapons system, complete with its own starfighter squadron, airspeeders and a command speeder.
Starhawk-class Battleship: As the first warship commissioned by the New Republic in the wake of the Battle of Endor, the Nadiri Dockyards Starhawk-class battleship represents both a hopeful future and a lamentable need for defense against the remnants of the Galactic Empire.
Starhawk Speeder Bike: A treid and true design, the Ikas-Adno Starhawk speeder bike is popular throughout the galaxy, utilized by everyone from smugglers and swoop gangs to merchants and moisture farmers.
Starlight-class Light Freighter: A joint venture of the venerable Rendili StarDrive and Surron StarTech, the Starlight-class light freighter is a hybrid of human and Surronian design and technology.
Starwind Pleasure Yacht: The Starwind-class pleasure yacht was originally Kuat Drive Yards' attempt to create a competitor for Corellian Engineering Corporation's YT-series.
Storm IV Cloud Car: The first high-atmosphere airspeeder to be classified as a cloud car, Bespin Motors' Storm IV was built for use by Cloud City's Bespin Wing Guard.
Strike Cruiser: Designed by Loronar, more traditionally known for their civilian exploration vessels, construction of the Strike-class Cruisers was licensed to the experience military shipwrights of Rendili Star Drive. The Strike-class was marketed towards the Empire as a flexible, multi-purpose ship. It is designed around a series of modular components, allowing the military garrisons, starfighter hangars or intelligence centres to be rapidly swapped in or out to serve any mission profile. On top of this modularity, the ship is surprisingly capable; pairing the speed and maneuverability of a light cruiser with the weaponry and shield of a heavy cruiser. This flexibility was not achieved without cost. The civilian experience of its designers led them to build the ship around a single set of spinal conduits, not the redundant system common in most military ships.
Subjugator-class Heavy Cruiser: More massive than even the Providence-class dreadnought, the Subjugator-class heavy cruiser is one of the largest if not the largest, warship currently at space.
Sunflare: Sunny's personal racing swoop is a heavily modified Mobquet Flare-S that she and Grinder have pushed nearly to the limits of its engineering.
Sunrunner zX Landspeeder: An armored variant of the civilian Sunrunner landspeeder produced by Ikas-Adno, the Sunrunner zX has seen limited success among militias and planetary security forces in the Mid and Outer Rim territories.
SuperHaul Model II Cargo Skiff: Used in numerous commercial and industrial applications, Ubrikkian Industries' SuperHaul II is a sturdy and reliable workhorse of a vehicle.
Surronian Conqueror-class Assault Ship: The Surronian Conqueror-class assault ship is exceedingly rare in the galaxy. Hull Type/Class: Light Assault Transport/Conqueror. Manufacturer: Surronian. T-6 Shuttle: First produced by the hives of Slayn & Korpil, the distinctively designed T-6 shuttle was a mainstay of the Jedi Order before and during the Clone Wars, when its strange circular design was often embossed with the emblem of the Galactic Republic
T-70 X-wing: The T-70 X-wing starfighter, also known as the T-70 X-wing fighter, was the successor to the T-65B X-wing starfighter, manufactured by Incom-FreiTek Corporation for use by the New Republic Defense Fleet. Faster and more expensive and complex than the former T-65B, the T-70 featured advanced weaponry and proved more versatile than its predecessor, and was essential in both dogfights and capital-ship scale combat operations. Four KX12 laser cannons offered single, dual, and quad firing modes. A built in dual proton torpedo launcher with quick-change magazines permitted the utilization of alternative armaments. New Republic demilitarization efforts and corruption would ultimately see production of the vessel shifted to several well-connected manufacturers. The T-70 would be eventually replaced by the newer T-85 X-wing.
T-85 X-wing: The T-85 represents the ultimate evolution of the general purpose starfighter that proved itself on every battlefront of the Galactic Civil War. Every micron of this ship was hand tailored for peak performance, and it represented the pride of the New Republic’s peace keeping fleet. The weapon control systems are fine tuned, the throttle responds to the deftest touch, and the seats are civilian-level comfort. Before the First Order unveiled themselves to the galaxy, most T-85s could be found with the New Republic Defense Fleet at Hosnian Prime. After the Hosnian cataclysm, these ships have become exceptionally hard to find as most were present with the New Republic’s fleet. Owning one and not reporting it to the First Order is a capital offense.
T2-E Petard-class Combat Engineering Veh: Whereas the AT-CE is a construction vehicle that can do a bit of fighting, the T2-E Petard is a fighting vehicle that can do a bit of construction work.
TIE Advanced v1: One of many experimental TIE designs tested and produced by Sienar Fleet Systems, the TIE Advanced v1 was designed to be a sturdy starfighter capable of working independently of fleet support.

TIE/ag \"Aggressor\" Starfighter: Introduced into the Imperial Navy just prior to the Battle of Yavin, the TIE/ag "Aggressor" is an advanced medium strike starfighter and one of a line of newly introduced advanced TIE-series variants.
TIE/ca \"Punisher\" Starfighter: Another advanced TIE-series variant developed in the wake of the Rebellion's stunning victory at Yavin, the TIE capital assault—more commonly known as "punisher"—is a heavy bomber used on missions to deliver massive payloads to places a Star Destroyer cannot venture.
TIE/d \"Defender\" Multi-role Starfighte: Designed in conjunction with Grand Admiral Thrawn's fighter initiative, the TIE/d shares many of the TIE series' design features—the spherical cockpit, the solar collectors in the wings, the twin ion engines—but beneath the relatively familiar skin is a completely different fighter.
TIE/fo: The TIE/fo space superiority fighter, commonly known as the First Order TIE fighter, was a starfighter used by the First Order. A product of Sienar-Jaemus Fleet Systems, the TIE/fo carried the appearance of its predecessor, the TIE/ln space superiority starfighter, though it featured internal advancements that provided it with greater defensive capabilities. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

TIE/sf: The Sienar-Jaemus Fleet Systems TIE/sf (Special Forces) Space Superiority Fighter represents a notable doctrine evolution compared to the classic Imperial Starfighter corps. Capable of prolonged operations away from its mothership, and operated by a pilot and co-pilot/tail gunner rather than a lone pilot. This fighter is typically deployed in hybrid commando/pilot operations pioneered by Commander Hask’s Inferno Squadron. The superior protection and communications suites the craft brings makes it an ideal fit for the chaotic nature of these special operations. The modest hyperdrive and rear-protecting guns help the /sf get in and get out efficiently without the need to call on larger fleet elements for support. Additionally, they are often fitted in a more modular manner to allow for customization to fit the mission profile, a stark contrast to the harshly enforced uniformity of old.

TIE/vn Silencer: The TIE/vn space superiority fighter, formally known as the TIE vendetta space superiority fighter and also known as the TIE silencer, was an angular variant of the TIE line starfighters used by the First Order. A product of Sienar-Jaemus Fleet Systems, its aggressive design and equally impressive firepower was specifically crafted for use among the most elite members of the First Order. As such, the fighter was engineered for speed and handling, though lesser pilots were still often overwhelmed and unable to maintain control of the nimble ship. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
TIE Auto-fighter: A rare tool in the Empire's arsenal, the TIE Auto-fighter, also knowns as the TIE Droid, is an automated starfighter. Whilst battle droid technology has been banned since the defeat of the Separatist droid armies in the Clone Wars, the Empire employs these illegal war machines primarily to defend installations where human pilots are impractical or a security risk- deep-space relays or secret research bases. Powered by a Cybot Galactica Ace-6 droid brain, the Auto-fighter is capable of enduring high-speed maneuvers that no organic fighter pilot could survive. Despite this, rigid programming means Auto-fighters still cannot match up to skilled organic pilots. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
TIE Bomber (First Order): The First Order TIE bomber was a series of bomber used by the First Order. The craft had two pods, similar to the old TIE bombers used by the Galactic Empire. The new version featured angular wings. The engine bank at the rear was squared off, creating a silhouette similar to the TIE silencer. The TIE variant had four solar collectors that also acted as its wings, with four laser cannons on each forward tip. The wings themselves were angled outwards, and it had two central pods, one with a cockpit. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Theta-class T-2c Shuttle: A special order by the Supreme Chancellor himself, the Theta-class was custom designed for the Republic and the plans never officially released for public sale. Maintaining the signature appearance of a Cygnus Spaceworks shuttle, the central wing was squatter and overslung the cockpit. Its compact frame reduced the number of passengers capable of travelling within, especially with the inclusion of the medical capsule. Designed to keep the chancellor alive and protected in an emergency evacuation, it was particularly well equipped for a shuttle. Traffic Interdiction Vessel: Appearing as an old freighter to sensors, and visually, this vessel was one of many models used for Traffic Interdiction Operations (TIOPS) for the SO BDE. It was heavily armed, capable of bringing down a large amount of ordnance onto a target, often coercing a surrender ahead of time simply by revealing their weapon systems. The goal of this freighter was to get within range of a vessel suspected of being a Separatist craft. These could be smugglers working for the CIS, or even a CIS’ own special operations mission attempting to go unnoticed. While the shuttle did not operate any ion weapons or assault boarding tubes, these were rarely necessary. There were however instances of these vessels being confused for pirates. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Trandoshan Dropship: The dagger-like and sharp shapes which form the profile of this bulky transport immediately convey the image of a predatory vessel. Produced by the Trandoshan Wasskah Shipyards, this odd transport certainly had a distinctive appearance—especially when landed. While on a planetary surface, the upper section of the vessel would raise, turning the cockpit into a sort of observation tower. Designed for the deployment of Trandoshan paramilitary forces, the ship was well armoured and capable of deploying troops on the move. However, it was not made to engage in firefights, often attempting to weather the attacking forces long enough to escape. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!

Treadable: Though continuous track propulsion is an extremely antiquated means of locomotion, its reliability in the face of harsh weather conditions and unstable terrain sees it in continuous use, particularly on impoverished worlds. The segmented track of a treadable is made of a composite manganese-plastoid alloy for lightweight durability. Spare track segments fill a repair hoppper aboard the treadable to replace any broken treads that would otherwise strand the vehicle. The durable tread crawls over most obstacles and gaps in terrain, letting the vehicle cross uneven stretches that would challenge lesser craft. Steering is achieved by turning one drive wheel at a different speed than the other. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Treadspeeder Biker: As the First Order blazes trails on harsh, untamed worlds in the unknown regions, it designed a rugged, reliable vehicle perfect for isolated scouting expeditions. Advancements in anti-repulsorlift technology also gave this new vehicle a tactical application. The treadspeeder is a hybrid design; a continuous track impeller drives the vehicle, and low-yield, jamming resistant repulsors are merely secondary systems that assist in stability and maneuvers.
Trexler Marauder: A more combat focused variant of the Imperial Troop Transport, the Trexler Marauder boasts improved durability and handling at the expense of cargo/passenger capacity. A somewhat recent addition to the Imperial arsenal, these vehicles went into production shortly after the battle of Hoth. After suffering crippling losses at the battles of Endor and Scariff the Imperial hierarchy shifted its focus to re-building the navy. As a result very few Marauders ever made it off the assembly line, making them something of a rarity. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Trident-class Assault Ship: Colicoid Creation Nest's Trident-class assault ship is one of the stranger vessels ever fielded by the CIS navy. Hull Type/Class: Assault Craft/Trident-class. Manufacturer: Colicoid Creation Nest. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One droid pilot, one droid co-pilot, two droid gunners. Encumbrance Capacity: 350. Passenger Capacity: 30 battle droids or aqua droids. Consumables: 72 hours. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits/8. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Four ventral-mounted articulated tentacles (Fire Arc All; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Engaged]; Linked 3, Tractor 2). *Eight hull-mounted light laser cannons (Fire Arc All; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]). *Ventral-mounted high-speed boarding drill (Fire Arc Ventral; Damage 10; Critical 2; Range [Engaged]; Breach 6, Sunder, Vicious 3).
Tsmeu-6 Personal Wheel Bike: This large, single-wheel bike allows a lone rider to quickly scope out an area, with its four additional legs deploying for continued movement over rough terrain, albeit at a reduced speed, when needed.
Turca 910 Special: The 910 Special, also known as the Turca Special, features modest performances and parts, and the factory Cooling on the 910 base model was so poor that when podracing pilot Toy Dampner installed a completely new one, it became standard on the design. Indeed, customizability being the main feature of the craft, it helped the model be improved over time like a good vintage, and alleviate the mediocre default attributes attributes of the frame. Sporting square, shield plates on top of both turbofan engines, the podracer gets a modicum of added protection against collisions, be it with the track environment, or with other podracers. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
UEV-M1 Marauder Engineer Squad Carrier: The Marauder engineer squad carrier by SoroSuub is a medium armored repulsorlift vehicle designed to carry a squad of twelve combat engineers and their attendant gear into the field with armor support.
UT-60D U-wing Troop Transport: Incom's UT-60D is a small personnel carrier and light attack craft marketed to planetary governments and private military concerns.
Ubrikkian Industries Hauler Air Skiff: The Hauler air skiff is a higher-altitude version of the ubiquitous Ubrikkian Industries cargo skiff. Vehicle Type/Model: Air skiff/Hauler. Manufacturer: Ubrikkian Industries. Maximum Altitude: 100 kilometers. Sensor Range: Short. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 100. Passenger Capacity: 20. Price/Rarity: 10,000 credits/4. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *None.
Ubrikkian Industries Kossak-class Frigat: Designed and constructed by Ubrikkian Industries specifically for the Hutts, the Kossak-class frigate allows the kajidics to protect their investments from rivals and pirates, as well as to project raw power when needed.
Ubrikkian Industries Pongeeta-class Swam: Based on the ancient fanboats used on Varl, the Pongeeta-classs swamp speeder is a light, maneuverable repulsorcraft typically used to traverse swamps and bogs.
Ubrikkian Industries Seltiss-2 Caravel: A high-end pleasure yacht with an equally high price-tag, the Seltiss-2 Caravel has enjoyed such success among the Hutts that it is unofficially known as the Hutt Caravel.
Ubrikkian Industries Talon I Combat Clou: A sturdy, nimble, high-atmosphere cloud car designed specifically for combat and patrol duty, the Ubrikkian Industries' Talon I is utilized by planetary security from the Core Worlds to the Outer Rim.
Ubrikkian TRL-03 SkyClaw: It is a universal truth that speeders and vehicles of all types break down and crash in highly inconvenient locations. Vehicle Type/Model: Airspeeder/TRL-03. Manufacturer: Ubrikkian Industries. Maximum Altitude: 1,000 meters. Sensor Range: Medium. Crew: One pilot, one tractor beam operator. Encumbrance Capacity: 1,000. Passenger Capacity: 2. Price/Rarity: 60,000 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 5. Weapons: *Turret-mounted light tractor beam (Fire Arc All; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Tractor 2). *Ventral light tractor beam (Fire Arc Down; Damage –; Critical –; Range [Close]; Tractor 2).
Umbaran Hover Tank: This weapon platform produced by the Umbaran Ghost Armaments was built around two large electromagnetic plasma cannons, the signature weapon used as heavy ordnance by the planet’s military. Similar in strength to a concussion missile and using the same technological concept as a plasma disruptor, the electromagnetic plasma cannon melts through armour and has tremendous explosive power. It was highly effective against the vehicles used by the Republic during the invasion of Umbara, but this particular platform was the most vulnerable of vehicles with this weapon. The hover tank was used as an assault vehicle, but its main weapons allowed it to act as close quarters artillery. Its armored cockpit protected the pilot from most smallarms, but its massive weapons made it vulnerable to flanking maneuvers as they were not well armoured and presented a visual blind spot.
Umbaran Mobile Heavy Cannon: The heaviest vehicle produced by Ghost Armaments, the MHC was a heavy walker used in the role of mobile artillery platform. It used the same unique heavy weapon found on the Umbaran manufactured starfighter and repulsor tank. Its heavy armour and shielding were used to prevent the walker from being destroyed as it got closer to the front lines. This was necessary, as its intuitive movement controls made it easy to walk right into enemy formations for particularly fanatic pilots. At these ranges, it could use its ventral laser cannons to eliminate infantry looking for weakspots (of which there were virtually none). Only ordnance from starships and other heavy vehicles could really penetrate its defenses. The MHC was a vehicle that either dominated the field or was quickly dispatched by opposing ordnance. This made its deployment require good intel of the situation to use adequately.
Undicur-class Jumpspeeder: These compact speeder bikes are little more than a low-output repulsor engine fitted with a saddle and rudimentary controls.
Unstable Terrain Artillery Transport: Known as the “Trident” to the clones which served alongside it, the UT-AT was quite a unique design from Kuat Drive Yards. This is likely due to the Mekuun Corporation’s involvement in its development. Despite the moniker of All Terrain, walkers were not truly best suited to all environments. Following a number of AT-TE losses from collapsing terrain, the Grand Army of the Republic commissioned a heavy weapons platform that could be used in the gaps left by walkers. The result was a craft with unique repulsor emitters never before seen. Mounting sixteen Mekuun Corporation repulsors in pairs on hydraulic pads, the UT-AT would be able to hover as a landspeeder despite its mass. The innova- tion was in its drive computer which automatically adjusted the repulsors to compensate for disturbances in the terrain beneath it and keep it smoothly trudging along.
Upsilon-class Command Shuttle: An Upsilon-class command shuttle, also referred to simply as an Upsilon-class shuttle or a First Order Command Shuttle, was a multi-purpose transport utilized by the First Order as an armored shuttle for high-class dignitaries and top-ranking military officials. Armed with twin heavy laser cannons and advanced sensor suites, the Upsilon-class shuttle was able to monitor and scan enemy communications to effectively avoid hostile encounters. Inspired by decades of Imperial shuttle design, it mimicked the Lambda-class T-4a shuttle, and was upgraded with powerful technologies resulting from the acquisition of chief scientists following the collapse of the Old Empire. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Urban Assault Triped Transport Walker: The design of the UA-TT began as a reimagining of the Clone Wars veteran, the All-Terrain Attack Pod. While the general form factor and tripedal nature were maintained, the artillery purpose of the AT-AP was replaced by that of urban warfare. Where the standard AT-ST would level streets and crush landspeeders underfoot, the forward-mounted articulated CAP-5 Captivator Arm allows the UA-TT much more precise movement, acting as a balancing implement. In addition to movement, the Captivator Arm also aids the pilot in separating walls from their structures and enemies from their footing. This is to say nothing of its modest yet still robust option of laser blaster and cannon emplacements to engage with enemies at range. To add to this, the rotating Spot Lamp opposite it’s blaster cannon cluster illuminates the darkness so that no subversive goes unnoticed.
V-19 Torrent: The V-19 Torrent is a sturdy, fast, long-range space superiority starfighter put into service by the Republic Navy at the outset of the war.
V-19b Dart Personal Flyer: Once a mainstay of military forces across the galaxy, the V-19 Torrent was eventually replaced by more versatile and heavily armored starfighters, and the V-19 fell out of use in all but the poorest, most backwater systems.
V-35 Courier: A common sight throughout the galaxy, the V-35 courier is a simple, durable craft useful in a variety of situations. Vehicle Type/Model: Landspeeder/V-35 or V-35 Courier. Manufacturer: SoroSuub Corporation or SoroSuub. Maximum Altitude: 0.5 meters or 1 meter. Sensor Range: Medium or Close. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 20. Passenger Capacity: 2 or 3. Price/Rarity: 5,500 or 7,500 credits/2. Customization Hard Points: 2 or 3. Weapons: *None.
V-4X-D Ski Speeder: The V-4X-D was once a luxury sportscraft constructed by Roche Industries for the purpose of high-stakes downhill asteroid races. Unfortunately, the entire sport fell out of favor after a group of racers were subdued by local fauna in a disastrous outing. Shortly after, these disused speeders were purchased and retrofitted and by the Rebel Alliances. They improved them by bolting on armor and strapping a pair of laser cannons on the far wing, giving the vehicle the look of an Alliance B-Wing, save for the fact it barely flies. The V-4X-D has repulsors and maneuvering thrusters, but it is rather clumsy in the air and doesn’t reach great heights. It shows it’s specialized sporting purpose in the way of the Roche Halofoil mono-ski, which allows the vehicle to skim the surface of the terrain for better stability and maneuverability.
V5 Medium Cargo Floater: These cheap but reliable gravsleds are ubiquitous on space stations and in starports throughout the galaxy. Hull Type/Class: V5-series. Manufacturer: TaggeCo. Maximum Altitude: 1 meter. Sensor Range: None. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 55. Passenger Capacity: 6 (without cargo). Price/Rarity: 1,225 credits/2. Customization Hard Points: 0. Weapons: *None.
VCX-100 Light Freighter: Corellian Engineering Corporation boasts a wide array of freighters that have proven tremendously popular, and the VCX-100 is yet another solid entry into the line.
VCX-series Auxiliary Starfighter: Hull Type/Class: Shuttle/VCX-series auxiliary starfighter. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None. Sensor Range: Close. Ship's Complement: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 5. Passenger Capacity: 8. Consumables: One day. Price/Rarity: 22,000 credits/6. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: * Forward-mounted twin light laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). * Dorsal turret-mounted light laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]).
VT-49 Decimator: A heavily armed transport and assault ship used primarily by the Imperial Navy, the VT-49 Decimator is a fearsome foe to smugglers and pirates who come under its guns.
Vanguard (vehicle): The Vanguard, nicknamed the V-Wheel, is a ground vehicle designed by Valkeri Enterprises for use exclusively by the Jedi Order. It’s the landbased equivalent of their Jedi Vector starfighters, with a smooth and sleek aesthetic. It uses six treads on its underside to move over rough terrain. Jedi outposts have at least one Vanguard in their standard kit, engineered to operate in the environments of the outposts' locations. To increase it’s adaptability, the Vanguard is able operate as a repulsorlift speeder as well as a wheeled transport. The Vanguard is capable of storing two Veil speeders within it. As with the Vector, the weapons systems of the Vanguard require a Jedi lightsaber to act as a key. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Veil V Speeder Bike: Manufactured exclusively for the Jedi Order by Valkeri Enterprises, the Veil is a high performance speeder bike. Roughly four metres long the Veil is, like most speeder bikes and swoops, little more than a powerful repulsor strapped to an incredibly light frame and saddle. The pilot controls its movements via two sets of handlebar mounted controls and two sets of footrest mounted controls. While the Veil is an extremely fast speeder, it is this speed that is its main vulnerability. One wrong twitch of the controls and the Veil is sent careening off-course. To keep the vessel as light as possible, all armour plating and weaponry have been removed from the Veil. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Venator-class Star Destroyer: The powerful Venator-class Star Destroyers are the backbone of the Republic Navy's carrier force. Hull Type/Class: Star Destroyer/Venator-class. Manufacturer: Kuat Drive Yards. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 1, Backup: Class 15. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: 7,400 officers, pilots, and enlisted crew. Starfighter Complement: 228 starfighters. Vehicle Complement: Numerous shuttles, walkers, landing craft, LAAT/i assault craft, and walkers. Encumbrance Capacity: 7,000. Passenger Capacity: 2,000 troops. Consumables: 2 years. Price/Rarity: 59,000,000 credits (R)/7. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *Four port and four starboard turret-mounted twin heavy turbolaser batteries (Fire Arc Forward, Port, and Aft or Forward, Starboard, and Aft; Damage 11; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Breach 4, Linked 1, Slow-Firing 2).
Vesper Dux Ultra-3: The Vesper Dux Ultra-3 wasn’t initially designed for podracing, but for battlefields on Ghishi, the native homeworld of Kraid Nemmeso. Unable to field the machine and compete, its designer and owner decided instead to adjust it for podracing competition. It remains a thickly armoured, slumbering beast of a podracer with oversized engines able to push surprisingly fast and profit from its power to reach top notch speeds. Everything tied to maneuverability and handling however, is an absolute afterthought, and the Vesper Dux is prone to fast overheat due to the amount of effort its engines have to produce in order to move it, and even moreso at these speeds. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Victory II–class Star Destroyer: The Victory-class Star Destroyer is an all-purpose heavy line-of-battle ship meant to support planetary landings and take part in fleet actions.
Victory–class Star Destroyer: Built during the height of the Clone Wars, the Victory-class was designed as an all-purpose heavy line-of-battle ship meant to support planetary landings and take part in fleet actions.
Vigil-class Corvette: Produced by Kuat Drive Yards for the Imperial Navy, the Vigil-class corvette is the smallest vessel in the iconic Destroyer class of ships.
Vindicator-class Heavy Cruiser: The Vindicator-class heavy cruisers were designed by Sienar Fleet Systems to take the place of the elderly Dreadnought-class heavy cruisers as the standard, multi-role fleet cruiser for the Imperial Navy.
Vokoff-Strood Plug 8G-927 Cluster Array: This podracer is powered by two cluster arrays, consisting of no less than sixteen engines (eight each). Probably one of the toughest podracers ever built, and besides a solid cooling system helped by the open air channel in the middle of each cluster, it performs very well in high speed variations with the help of its multiple engines, all shaped differently and acting as a ‘gearbox’ of sorts, able to adjust to any speed variation. However this craft has very little else to show for and its awkward profile hinders its speed potential. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Vokoff-Strood Titan 2150 Rocket: The Titan 2150 is an incredibly fast podracer, its two massive high-pass turbofan engines bringing the speed and boost package needed to achieve those breakneck speeds. It is found severely lacking in maneuverability and takes time spooling up however. It became apparent that the injection system was finicky when famous pilot Ratts Tyerell jammed the accelerator of one of the engines of his own model, the Scatalpen, Scatalpen, leading to a lethal crash in the Laguna Caves of the Boonta Eve Classic. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Vulptereen 327: A very sturdy podracer produced on Vulpter for the famous vulptereen racer Dud Bolt, able to endure more than a beating and hold all manners of strenuous situations very well, the 327 comes equipped with a surprisingly good track grip, solid braking, good speed, but no real maneuverability. With not a lot more to show for, the podracer has nevertheless been called an incredible machine by famous podracing annoncer Fodedesinbeed Annodue This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Vulture-class Droid Starfighter: Officially designated the Variable Geometry Self-Propelled Battle Droid Mk I, the Vulture-class droid starfighter is the most common aerospace superiority fighter in the CIS's arsenal.
WLO-5 Light Speeder Tank: The WLO-5 is a relatively uncommon light infantry support tank produced by Ubrikkian Ord Pedrovia, a subsidiary of Ubrikkian Transports.
WTK-85A Interstellar Transport: Designed for scouts and lone pilots, Sublight Products Corporation's WTK-85A is one of the finest examples of a long-range reconnaissance ship on the market today. Considered by many Core World designers as a second- class, backwater manufacturer, Sublight Products Corporation (SPC) was founded just under a century ago by a group of Trandoshan shipwrights. Despite the snobbish attitude of designers at Kuat, CEC or Fondor, SPC nevertheless have developed a strong range of high-quality and reasonably-priced starships. SPC got their start working jointly with Incom Corporation on their incredibly successful Z-95 Headhunter range. The two companies further expanded on this partnership with the successful ARC-170 starfighter, which debuted during the early days of the Clone Wars.
Wander-class Jump Freighter: Built specifically to compete with Corellian Engineering Corporation's HWK-series freighters, Wander-class freighters have been plying the spacelanes since the Clone Wars.
Wayfarer-class Medium Freighter: Kuat Systems Engineering's Wayfarer-class is a relatively new entry to the medium transport market. Hull Type/Class: Freighter/Wayfarer-class. Manufacturer: Kuat Systems Engineering. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 14. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot, one engineering, one cargo master, six crew. Encumbrance Capacity: 850. Passenger Capacity: 6. Consumables: Three months. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 5. Weapons: *Dorsal quad laser cannon (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Accurate 1, Linked 3). While the Edge of the Empire Roleplaying Game: Core Rulebook does not show a Sensor Range, the Errata (Page 2) corrects this to "Sensor Range: Short".
Wellos & Allos 825: A craft distinguished for its hefty accelerator and low to average top speed, the Wellos & Allos 825 also proves easy to maneuver and displays a sizeable combustion chamber. Thanks to the design of the radiator fins attached to the engines, the podracer is able to cool down pretty well at the expense of a wider silhouete, and proves unusually durable for its average size. The overall concept focuses best on track performances, but leaves all the support systems to be somewhat desired, notably its cheap repair module. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Wheel Security Starfighters: The Wheel has a collection of commonly available starfighters for their patrols and police actions. Vehicle Type/Model: Starfighter/Z-95-AF4 Headhunter. Manufacturer: Incom/Subpro. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None. Sensor Range: Close. Ship's Complement: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 8. Passenger Capacity: 0. Consumables: One day. Price/Rarity: 85,000 credits (R)/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Wingtip Mounted Light Laser Cannons (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1). *Forward Mounted Concussion Missile Launchers (Fire Arc Forward; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Breach 4, Blast 4, Guided 3, Limited Ammo 6, Linked 1, Slow-Firing 1).
Whelik All-Terrain Explorer: Designed and produced by Corellian Mining Corporation to fill the need for a civilian vehicle capable of penetrating the most dense, inhospitable terrain, the Whelik has six high-arching legs that give the vehicle its distinctive silhouette while at the same time making it ideal for accessing rugged, nearly impossible-to-reach locations on remote planets.
World's End Station: Over two centuries old, the station was first established as a frontier asteroid mining operation by independent prospectors. As the number of mines grew and tibanna gas harvesters arrive in-system, the earliest and most well-established mining station gradually developed from a nearly-automated installation crewed by a few hardy shift workers, to a small outpost offering repairs and R&R, to a full-blown station with thousands of permanent inhabitants and families. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
X-34 Landspeeder: One of the most popular landspeeders in the galaxy, SoroSuub's X-34 has been in production in one form or another for decades.
XL-3c Guardsman Patrol Airspeeder: The XL-3c Guardsman, produced by Tion Mil/Sci Industries, is the civilian version of TMSI's XL-5 Guardian force reconnaissance ship used by the Imperial Army and the ISB.
XP-40 Luxury Coach: SoroSuub Corporation's XP-40 is the massive Sullustan conglomerate's entry into the luxury landspeeder market, catering to the wealth and powerful throughout the galaxy.
XT Beetle Transport: The XT Beetle was the solution Baktoid Armour Workshop to the MTT’s inability to function in a range of environments. Instead of using the reliable yet temperamental repulsorlifts which were already being stretched in lifting such a heavy vehicle, they used 10 pairs of mechanical legs. The engineers also developed the hull into a larger size and took the opportunity to improve on the MTT design. This resulted in a craft much larger, one that could serve as a command centre after the deployment of its carried forces. Republic forces only rarely see an XT Beetle in combat, and often only when the CIS is on their back legs and have been pushed back to their command post. They aren’t particularly capable of combat, but are heavily armoured so that they can be part of an initial landing in a new battle theater. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Xanter-class Deep-Space Exploration Frig: The Xanter-class deep-space exploration frigate is an ancient vessel. Hull Type/Class: Firgate/Xanter. Manufacturer: Rendilli Stardrive. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 3, Backup: Class 9, Tertiary backup: Class 18. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: 150 officers, pilots, and crew. Starfighter Complement: Six starfighters. Encumbrance Capacity: 2,000, depending on configuration. Passenger Capacity: 50 scientists and explorers. Comsumables: Two-and-a-half years. Price/Rarity: 3,750,000 credits/7. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: *Two forward-, three port-, and three starboard-mounted heavy laser cannons (Fire Arc Forward or Port or Starboard; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]).
Xi-class Light Shuttle: The First Order employed the Xi-class light shuttle for many purposes, including ferrying officers and transporting equipment. Xi-class shuttles also had light weapons and minimal shields, relying instead on starfighter escorts for protection. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Xiytiar-class Heavy Transport: Known primarily as a manufacturer of military starships and fighters, TransGalMeg also produces the massive, lumbering Xiytiar-class heavy transports.
Y164 Slave Transport: A common sight in the skies above Nar Shaddaa and Sleheyron, these ominous vessels are used to transport slaves to and from planets throughout the galaxy.
YC-123B Transport Hauler: The YC-123B transport hauler was a model of transport manufactured by the Corellian Engineering Corporation. A troop transport, the YC-123B transport hauler could rapidly deploy up to 120 passengers (Or a couple dozen cavalry units) onto the battlefield via a large carrying compartment under the cockpit. It was armed with two CEC FP-35 forward heavy laser cannons, one on each side of the ship, as well as a CEC AG-3M triple laser cannon turret aft of the cockpit. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
YG-4210 Light Freighter: Among the oldest ships still plying the hyperspace lanes, Corellian Engineering Corporation's YG-4210 is a throwback to another generation of ship design.
YG-4400 Light Freighter: Although the design is almost 50 years old, the YG-4400 still holds a sentimental place in most Corellians' hearts. Hull Type/Class: Light Freighter/YG-4400. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 1/Backup: Class 18. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot/gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 75. Passenger Capacity: 2. Consumables: One month. Price/Rarity: 110,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *Aft concussion missile launcher (Fire Arc Aft; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Blast 4, Breach 4, Guided 3, Limited Ammo 5, Slow-Firing 1).
YKL-37R Nova Courier: The last ship produced by Gallofree Yards before its bankruptcy, the YKL-37R Nova Courier was a last ditch effort to save the struggling starship manufacturer.
YQ-400 Monitor-Class System Patrol Ship: The Monitor-class system patrol ship is a droid operated vessel marketed to planetary and system governmental bodies. Skills: Cool 2, Gunnery 2, Piloting (Space) 3, Vigilance 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Multi-tasking Control (on its turn, the Monitor Droid may perform one Pilot-only maneuver and one Pilot-only action, then perform a number of additional actions needed to fire each of its functioning weapons once). Hull Type/Class: Droid Operated Patrol Vessel/Monitor-class. Manufacturer: The Nerfworks. Hyperdrive: None. Navicomputer: None. Sensor Range: Long. Ship's Complement: None, droid operated. Encumbrance Capacity: None. Passenger Capacity: None. Consumables: Three Months. Price/Rarity: 200,000 credits/8. Customization Hard Points: 0. Weapons: *Dorsal Turret Mounted Twin Linked Medium Laser Cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1).
YT-1000 Light Freighter: The third ship in Corellian Engineering's enduring YT-series of modular light freighters, the YT-1000 was the first to gain any popularity in the galaxy.
YT-1200 Light Freighter: Built to replace Corellian Engineering Corporation's revolutionary YT-1000 modular light freighter, the YT-1200 was supposed to redefine the light freighter concept.
YT-1210 Light Freighter: An older, reliable light freighter, the YT-1210 is a variant of another freighter in the YT-series, the 1200. Hull Type/Class: Freighter/YT-1210. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 16. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one gunner. Encumbrance Capacity: 100. Passenger Capacity: 4. Consumables: Two months. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 3. Weapons: *Dorsal turret-mounted medium laser cannon (Fire Arc All; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]). The YT-1250 is little more than an upgraded YT-1210. To represent a YT-1250, use the YT-1210 profile with a cost of 130,000 credits and the following changes: * Increase armor to 3. * Reduce encumbrance capacity to 80.
YT-1300 Light Freighter: Perhaps the most common light freighter in service today, Corellian Engineering Corporation's YT-1300 is the light freighter all others are measured against.
YT-1760 Small Transport: Released roughly thirty years before the Battle of Yavin, the YT-1760 is a small, fast, and agile light freighter in the vein of the older HWK-series.
YT-2000 Light Freighter: Intended to be the pinnacle of the YT-series, the YT-2000 is by any measure an impressive light freighter. Hull Type/Class: Freighter/YT-2000. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 12. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot/engineer. Encumbrance Capacity: 170. Passenger Capacity: 7. Consumables: Three months. Price/Rarity: 120,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 5. Weapons: * Dorsal and ventral turret-mounted twin medium laser cannons (Fire Arc All; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Close]; Linked 1).
YT-2400 Light Freighter: Corellian Engineering's YT-2400 light freighters are an update of the aging YT-1300 class. Hull Type/Class: Freighter/YT-2400. Manufacturer: Corellian Engineering Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: Class 12. Navicomputer: Yes. Sensor Range: Short. Ship's Complement: One pilot, one co-pilot/engineer. Encumbrance Capacity: 140. Passenger Capacity: 6. Consumables: Two months. Price/Rarity: 130,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 5. YT Dart: One of the many optional add-ons for the freighter, the YT Dart is an auxiliary tug that fits neatly between the cargo mandibles of the YT-1300.
YV-560 Light Freighter: Designed with planetary exploration in mind, the YV-560 is geared toward providing a stable and reliable platform from which explorers and archaeologists can base their remote research.
YV-666 Light Freighter: Slab-sided and imposing, Corellian Engineering Corporation's YV-666 light freighter is a radical departure from the company's traditional styling principles.
YV-929 Light Freighter: A rarity among the Corellian Engineering Corporation's YV-series, which primarily consists of cargo vessels, the YV-929 was designed from the keel up as an armed transport.
YZ-775 Medium Transport: The YZ-775 is a popular medium transport used by independent traders, smugglers and mercenaries across the galaxy. Hull Type/Class: Transport/YZ-775. Living Coral Hull: The starship is made out of living material and is almost its own sentient organism. As such, it can heal itself overtime.
Z-28 Skywing: Produced in vast numbers by the Incom Corporation, the Z-28 Skywing is one of the most common starfighters in service in local security forces and defence groups. The Republic is under direct control of a number of Skywings, which it uses for VIP escort duty and support tasks while further squadrons of Skywings are owned by the fleets of many member worlds of the Republic Defence Coalition. The small fighter sports an arrowhead-shaped profile. With rear mounted twin sublight engines, that feature Incom’s proprietary fuisal thrust design, the small fighter boasts a not inconsiderable top speed. The Skywing also features two underslung, forward-facing RX Defender laser cannons. A small shielding unit to the rear of the starfighter gives the vessel extra protection, compensating for its slightly below average armouring.
Z-6 Jetpack: When a soldier needs to make a swift and unexpected arrival, a jetpack provides one of the quickest and most surprising methods.
Z-95-AF4-H \"Heavy-95\" Multi-Role Starf: A variant of the old, reliable Z-95 Headhunter, the Heavy-95 is the result of a recent service life extension program for Headhunters serving in the Rebel Alliance.
Z-95-AF4 Headhunter: Designed in a joint venture between Incom Corporation and Subpro Corporation during the early days of the Clone Wars, the tiny Z-95 was considered one of the most capable starfighters of its day.
Z-TIE: The Z-TIE is a starfighter colloquially known as an "ugly"—an amalgamation of salvaged snubfighter parts reconstructed into a single starfighter.
ZH-25 Questor Light Freighter: Spurred by the success of the Z-10 Seeker-class scout ship, the ZH-25 Questor-class is Starfeld Industries' first foray into the light freighter market.
Zenuas 33 Starfighter: Another unique system defence fighter, the Zenuas 33 is a product of the Umbaran manufacturer Ghost Armaments. Fitting an array of advanced technology seen nowhere else in the galaxy, this Umbaran fighter is superior to most widely produced starfighters, though lacks any space for further modifications. The lack of a hyperdrive and its odd avionics interface would also hamper its entry into the wider galactic market—if the Umbarans ever sought to commercialise the craft. Appearing like a pelvic bone of a great beast found on Umbara’s ever-night planet, the Zenuas 33 airframe was capable of tight turns and high speeds thanks to its light construction. It relied on powerful rayshielding to protect the craft against destruction and the combination of the flexible frame with the special avionics interface allowed for an extremely maneuverable fighter while maintaining high speeds.
Zephyr-G Swoop: First produced by Mobquet in the decades leading up to the Clone Wars, the Zephyr-G is a relative oddity among swoops. Vehicle Type/Model: Swoop/Zephyr-G. Manufacturer: Mobquet Swoops and Speeders. Maximum Altitude: 6 meters. Crew: One pilot. Encumbrance Capacity: 4. Passenger Capacity: 1. Price/Rarity: 5,700 credits/4. Customization Hard Points: 2. Weapons: *None.
Zephyr GB-134 Speeder: Anyone attempting to evade the law in Canto Bight had best move fast, as police employ swift, lightweight GB-134 pursuit craft, also known as jet-sticks. The nimble craft are well suited to navigating the narrow roadways of the Old Town. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Zeta-class Heavy Cargo Shuttle: The Zeta-class shuttle was designed as a short-haul heavy lifter capable of carrying large cargoes in its highly secure hold.
